description:
About the job

This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Marketing Operations Administrator based in Mexico.

This is a high-impact operational role designed for a highly organized professional who thrives in fast-paced, detail-critical environments. You will act as the backbone of marketing operations, ensuring that logistics, budgeting, vendor coordination, and administrative systems run seamlessly across multiple initiatives. Working closely with senior leadership, you will take ownership of day-to-day execution support, freeing up strategic capacity for executive stakeholders. The role spans marketing operations, event coordination, financial tracking, and back-office systems management, requiring both precision and adaptability. You will contribute directly to the smooth execution of events, campaigns, and operational workflows across a multi-brand organization. Ideal for someone who enjoys structure, problem-solving, and ownership, this position combines autonomy with high visibility and real operational impact.

Accountabilities


Act as a direct operational support to senior marketing leadership, managing action items, preparing briefing materials, tracking deliverables, and ensuring follow-through on priorities.
Oversee marketing financial operations including budget tracking, invoice processing, vendor payments, expense reconciliation, and maintenance of spend and SaaS cost controls.
Manage end-to-end inventory, print, and fulfillment processes, including sourcing branded materials, coordinating shipments, tracking delivery timelines, and overseeing stock management.
Coordinate procurement and vendor management activities, ensuring contracts, invoices, and service agreements are properly tracked and maintained.
Support back-office operations including document management, scheduling coordination, access provisioning, and administrative system upkeep.
Plan and execute marketing events and conferences, including logistics such as venue booking, catering, travel arrangements, booth setup, AV coordination, and on-site execution.
Support internal teams by ensuring operational readiness across campaigns, events, and marketing initiatives with no disruption to timelines or deliverables.
Maintain operational dashboards, spreadsheets, and tracking systems to ensure full visibility over marketing execution and financial flows.


Requirements


7+ years of experience in marketing operations, executive support, operations management, or a logistics-heavy administrative role with ownership of budgets and projects.
Strong experience in event planning and execution, including conferences, booths, vendor coordination, travel logistics, and on-site operations.
Solid understanding of financial operations such as invoice processing, expense reconciliation, GL coding, and budget vs. actual tracking.
Advanced proficiency in Google Workspace, spreadsheets, and operational tools; familiarity with SaaS systems, billing processes, and digital workflows.
Experience managing procurement, vendor relationships, print production, fulfillment, and shipping logistics.
Highly organized, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple deadlines without losing accuracy or control over execution.
Strong ownership mindset with a proactive, self-sufficient approach to problem-solving and task execution.
Comfortable leveraging AI tools and automation to improve workflows, streamline operations, and enhance efficiency.
Strong written and spoken English communication skills, with the ability to work effectively in a remote international environment.
Valid passport and willingness to travel internationally for on-site events (up to several times per year).
Experience supporting senior executives or acting in an executive assistant / operations hybrid capacity is highly desirable.


Benefits


Fully remote working arrangement based in LATAM with flexible coordination across international teams.
Competitive monthly compensation of USD $2,500.
Opportunity to work closely with senior leadership in a high-ownership operational role.
Exposure to large-scale marketing operations, real estate investment branding, and legacy-focused initiatives.
Hands-on involvement in end-to-end event execution, including international conferences and on-site activations.
Access to modern operational tools and AI-driven workflows to improve efficiency and automation.
Professional growth through high-responsibility work and direct executive exposure.
Occasional international travel for events and conferences.
Opportunity to build and optimize real operational systems with tangible business impact.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

 Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

description:
About the job

Donaldson is committed to solving the world’s most complex filtration challenges. Together, we make cool things. As an established technology and innovation leader, we are continuously evolving to meet the filtration needs of our changing world. Join a culture of collaboration and innovation that matters and a chance to learn, effect change, and make meaningful contributions at work and in communities.

At Donaldson, we are committed to solving the world's most complex filtration challenges. Together, we make cool things.

The Marketing Specialist I will play a key role in executing trade shows and events, digital marketing campaigns, marketing automation programs, lead management activities, and content localization efforts that support customer engagement, demand generation, and business growth. This position works closely with sales, product management, engineering, and regional marketing teams to ensure effective marketing execution and alignment with business objectives.

Key Responsibilities


Support the planning and execution of regional trade shows, customer seminars, webinars, and industry events, including logistics, promotional materials, and lead management activities.
Coordinate event promotion and post-event follow-up efforts in partnership with sales teams to maximize customer engagement and business opportunities.
Develop and execute digital marketing campaigns, including email marketing, lead nurturing programs, and marketing automation workflows.
Support audience segmentation, campaign deployment, and optimization efforts through performance monitoring and data analysis.
Create, update, translate, and localize customer-facing content, including emails, landing pages, web content, and digital marketing assets.
Build and maintain landing pages and web forms, ensuring effective integration with CRM and marketing automation platforms.
Support lead tracking, routing, and lifecycle management processes to ensure accurate data and effective lead conversion.
Monitor and report on campaign, lead, and event performance metrics, providing insights and recommendations for continuous improvement.
Collaborate with sales, product management, engineering, and regional marketing teams to ensure alignment, technical accuracy, and successful execution of marketing initiatives.
Support regional marketing activities across Mexico, Brazil, and North America, contributing to customer engagement, demand generation, and business growth.


Minimum Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Business Administration, or a related field.
1–3 years of experience in marketing, digital marketing, communications, or a related role.
Experience with CRM systems and marketing automation platforms.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience supporting B2B, industrial, manufacturing, or technical marketing environments.
Familiarity with marketing automation platforms such as Marketo, HubSpot, Eloqua, or similar tools.
Brazilian Portuguese language skills.


Additional Information


This position is based in Aguascalientes, Mexico.
The role supports marketing activities across Mexico, with opportunities to collaborate on initiatives throughout Brazil and North America.
The position operates within a highly collaborative, matrixed global organization.


Temporary Assignment Notice

This is a temporary position expected to last approximately four (4) months and is intended to provide maternity leave coverage.

Employment opportunities for positions in the United States may require use of information which is subject to the export control regulations of the United States. Hiring decisions for such positions are required by law to be made in compliance with these regulations. Applicants for employment opportunities in other countries must be able to meet the comparable export control requirements of that country and of the United States.

Donaldson Company has been made aware that there are several recruiting scams that are targeting job seekers. These scams have attempted to solicit money for job applications and/or collect confidential information, Donaldson will never solicit money during the application or recruiting process. Donaldson only accepts online applications through our Careers | Donaldson Company, Inc. website and any communication from a Donaldson recruiter would be sent using a donaldson.com email address. If you have any questions about the legitimacy of an employment opportunity, please reach out to talentacquisition@donaldson.com to verify that the communication is from Donaldson.

Our policy is to provide equal employment opportunities to all qualified persons without regard to race, gender, color, disability, national origin, age, religion, union affiliation, sexual orientation, veteran status, citizenship, gender identity and/or expression, or other status protected by law.

description:
About the job

Hi there! We are South and our client is looking for a Google Ads / PPC Specialist!

Note To Applicants


Eligibility: This position is open to candidates residing in Latin America. 
Application Language: Please submit your CV in English. Applications submitted in other languages will not be considered. 
Professional Presentation: We encourage you to showcase your professional experience by including a Loom video in the application form. While this is optional, candidates who provide a video presentation will be given priority. 
Interview Policy: The use of artificial intelligence (AI) tools during interviews are strictly prohibited. 
Note: As part of the final stages of our hiring process, candidates may be asked to provide professional references for a reference check. If hired, you may also be asked to update your LinkedIn profile :)


The Role

We’re hiring a hands-on Google Ads / PPC Specialist focused on lead generation through paid search. The goal is simple: turn high-intent searches into phone calls and booked appointments, not clicks or impressions.

This is not e-commerce or ROAS optimization on product catalogs. Success here means driving lower cost per lead, higher lead volume, and stronger lead quality across home services and local service industries such as HVAC, plumbing, roofing, electrical, and healthcare.

You’ll own your accounts end to end — from audits and campaign builds to optimization, scaling, and performance storytelling. You’ll collaborate closely with a copywriter (ads), a web/UX team (landing pages), and account managers (client context and communication).

What You’ll Own


Audit, build, manage, and scale Google Ads campaigns (Search, Local Services Ads / Google Guaranteed, Performance Max, 
Display, and remarketing) across multiple accounts
Improve performance by reducing cost per lead while increasing lead volume and quality
Manage keyword strategy, including match types, search term analysis, negative keywords, and continuous optimization to eliminate wasted spend
Own conversion tracking integrity using GA4, Google Tag Manager, and call tracking tools (e.g., CallRail or similar), ensuring accuracy before optimization
Close the loop with CRM data by feeding qualified leads and booked jobs back into Google Ads via offline conversion imports
Manage bidding strategies and budget allocation to maximize lead efficiency
Align landing pages with ad intent to improve conversion rates (in collaboration with web/UX teams)
Continuously test ad copy, landing pages, bidding strategies, and audiences
Report performance clearly to stakeholders and proactively flag risks or performance shifts
Use AI tools daily to accelerate analysis, reporting, and optimization workflows


The Hard Parts (Read Before Applying)


We prefer candidates self-selecting out if the environment is not a fit:
Lead generation is different from e-commerce. This role focuses on lead quality, not purchase ROAS. Interpreting lead quality requires deeper judgment than transaction-based metrics
Full ownership role. You will both execute campaigns and own outcomes — there is no separate team running accounts for you
Multiple accounts, fast pace. You’ll manage several clients simultaneously, each with different seasonality patterns and performance dynamics
Client communication under pressure. You’ll handle questions when lead volume fluctuates or costs rise, and must be able to explain performance clearly and calmly
Tracking accuracy is critical. Incorrect tracking invalidates all optimization work — validation is a core responsibility


How You’ll Be Measured


Cost per lead (CPL) — reduction and stability across accounts
Lead volume and quality — increased number of qualified leads (calls, booked appointments, form fills)
Client retention — accounts remain due to consistent performance
Account health — clean structure, accurate tracking, proper negatives, and well-documented changes


Who You Are

This role is a strong fit if you:


Have 3+ years of experience managing Google Ads / paid search with measurable results
Can independently audit accounts and prioritize fixes with clarity
Have scaled budgets while maintaining cost-per-lead targets
Have deep expertise in Search campaigns (match types, search term mining, negatives, bidding strategies)
Have experience with Local Services Ads (Google Guaranteed), including setup, optimization, and lead dispute handling
Have implemented and managed conversion tracking (GA4, GTM, call tracking) independently
Have experience feeding offline CRM conversions back into Google Ads (closed-loop attribution)
Are highly data-driven and continuously testing new approaches
Communicate performance clearly and confidently in client-facing settings
Use AI tools (e.g., Claude, ChatGPT or similar) as part of your daily workflow
Are fluent in professional English and comfortable working with U.S.-based teams and clients


Schedule: Monday to Friday, 9 AM – 5 PM U.S. Central Time.

Compensation: USD salary

Location: 100% remote opportunity

If this opportunity sounds good to you, send us your resume!

description:
About the job

About Klar

Let’s start with the basics! Klar is a Mexican fintech whose mission is to democratize and revolutionize how financial services are delivered in Mexico — especially since half of the population still doesn’t have a bank account. Since our launch in 2019, we’ve served more than 7 million users with a growing suite of debit and credit products, and the future is brighter than ever.

Today, we’re not only scaling our consumer business, but also building Klar Empresas (B2B) from the ground up — a new division focused on empowering Mexican SMEs with the financial tools, capital, and services they need to grow. This is an exciting new chapter where we get to design and build from scratch while leveraging the trust and scale of Klar’s platform.

We are also proud to have been certified as a “Great Place to Work” in Mexico — something we’ve worked hard to achieve and will continue to build on.

Our people

If you join us at Klar, you’ll be welcomed to a team which is rich in many talents and we are very proud! With our head office in Mexico City, and remote tech hubs in Berlin and Argentina, we are always learning something new about another culture or language. With so many people from different backgrounds and walks of life (young professionals, parents, LGBTQ+, neurodivergence), you’ll definitely find your people here!

Our values


Ownership - We own our successes & our failures as a team.
Excellence - We do everything to the best of our ability & always seek to achieve a new level of excellence in our work.
Inclusion - We believe we are stronger together and actively work to promote a safe, diverse, inclusive, and respectful culture.
Customer Obsession - We understand the value Klar can bring to its customers & it’s always at the forefront of our decisions.
Klarity - We communicate clearly & with authenticity. It’s in our name & it’s what we do.


The position and your daily adventures

We’re looking for a Performance Marketing Specialist to execute, optimize, and scale paid acquisition campaigns across Google, Meta, and other performance platforms. This is a hands-on role for someone who loves data, experimentation, and turning budgets into measurable growth.

You will own the day-to-day operation of performance campaigns, continuously improving CAC, ROAS, and funnel conversion, working closely with creative, analytics, and growth teams. You’ll be part of a high-performance team managing a US$2M+ monthly budget.

What you can expect:


Reporting: Create weekly and ad-hoc performance reports. Make financial analysis of campaign spend.
Own campaign tracking, attribution setup, and pixel/event health.
Operate and optimize paid campaigns: Constant monitoring and daily adjustments of paid ads campaigns on Google, Facebook and new channels.
Launch new campaigns, ad sets, creatives, and targeting strategies
Monitor performance daily and implement data-driven optimizations.
Run A/B tests on creatives, audiences, landing pages, and funnels.
Collaborate with creative and product teams to improve conversion.
KEY KPIs: New users and approved credit lines, Cost per Acquisition (CpA), and ROAS. 


What we are looking for:


Bachelor's degree in Industrial Engineering, Business Engineering, Digital Transformation Engineering, (or similar) with an orientation to data analysis.
2+ years hands-on experience running performance campaigns on: Meta and Google.
Strong analytical skills, comfort working with performance dashboards and experience with SQL.
Experience with conversion tracking, pixels, and attribution tools.
Ability to do Data-driven decision-making.
High ownership, attention to detail, and execution speed.
Advanced English level.
Experience in fintech, apps, or subscription products is a plus.


Our offer to you:


Competitive salary based on performance and experience
Chance of earning Klar stock options
15 days of paid vacation per year; plus extended maternity and paternity leaves
Vacation premium
Food vouchers
30 days of Christmas bonus
Medical Insurance
Computer devices
Well hub subscription to offer mental and physical health 
Sponsored coaching and therapy sessions 
A modern centrally located office in Mexico City with free drinks, snacks, and regular social events
International work environment with amazing and highly skilled people
A world-class team that helps you evolve your skills in areas you're interested in


Klar is a safe place for everyone!

We trust our highly skilled and diverse team, and we’re committed to creating a welcoming and inclusive environment for new talents to flourish. We value diversity and welcome all applications regardless of gender, nationality, ethnic and social origin, religion/belief, physical abilities, age, sexual orientation and identity.

Should you require any accommodations through the recruitment process, please don’t hesitate to let us know how we can help!

title: Technical Implementation Specialist (Web Analytics)
skills: Guiding Fullstory onboarding customers throughout the onboarding project, from kick-off to final training session.
Coaching on installation, answering basic questions, sharing best practices, and providing high-quality, live-instructor training of the Fullstory platform.

Building and reviewing presentation materials for client meetings, keeping accurate and detailed records/notes of training sessions, and communicating professionally to clients and team members.
Completion of 4-year college degree in Human Computer Interaction (HCI), UX Research, Digital Marketing, Digital Analytics, or other related field.
6+ months of web analytics experience using tools such as Google Analytics, Fullstory, and similar tools.

title: Trafficker Digital / Especialista en Paid Media
description:
About the job

Sobre Backdoor Marketing

Somos una agencia de marketing digital que maneja múltiples cuentas. Buscamos un(a) Trafficker Digital que quiera crecer en pauta digital al lado de un equipo con experiencia.




IMPORTANTE — Esquema de colaboración:

Esta es una colaboración como PROFESIONAL INDEPENDIENTE (prestación de servicios profesionales), no una contratación como empleado interno. Tú emites factura (CFDI) por tus servicios. Buscamos personas que puedan facturar o estén dispuestas a darse de alta en el SAT; te orientamos en el proceso.




Responsabilidades:

· Creación, configuración, monitoreo y optimización de campañas en Google Ads y Meta Ads (también LinkedIn y TikTok) para 8 a 10 cuentas.

· Optimización diaria: presupuestos, pujas, públicos y rendimiento. Los creativos los entrega el Content Manager; tú te enfocas en performance.

· Medición y reportes: Google Analytics 4, Google Tag Manager, eventos y conversiones, con métricas clave (CPA, ROAS, CTR).

· Trabajo cercano con el equipo de contenido.




Requisitos:

· 1+ año en pauta digital (Google y Meta Ads), o certificaciones más práctica demostrable.

· Mentalidad analítica y orientación a resultados.

· Manejo de herramientas de IA/automatización para optimizar a escala.

· Autonomía y organización: trabajas por objetivos y entregables.

· Capacidad de emitir CFDI o disposición a darte de alta en RESICO.




Lo que ofrecemos:

· Colaboración como profesional independiente (tú facturas tus servicios).

· Mejor ingreso neto: bajo el régimen RESICO el ISR es de 1 a 2.5%, mucho menor que las retenciones de un sueldo.

· Esquema híbrido: trabajas principalmente desde casa, con visitas ocasionales a oficina o cliente.

· Libertad para manejar otros clientes.

· Pago mensual competitivo más bonos por desempeño.




Para postularte, comparte tu CV y, si tienes, casos o cuentas que hayas manejado.

description:
About the job

GenBI Engineer (Generative Business Intelligence Engineer)

Location: CDMX / Remote

Kohler Opportunity

Role Summary:

Join Kohler’s AI Innovation team – Enterprise Architecture Office team to design and deliver Generative Business Intelligence (GenBI) / Conversational AI solutions that enable trusted, self service analytics and AI assisted insights. As a GenBI Engineer I, you will focus on enterprise data modeling, certified data assets, and metadata enrichment, while building conversational and GenAI powered BI experiences using Databricks Genie, Microsoft Copilot and/or Fabric Data Agents.

You will work extensively with Databricks, Unity Catalog, and/or Microsoft Fabric - One Lake, contributing to standardized semantic models aligned with the Common Data Model (CDM). The role emphasizes data trust, governance, discoverability, and AI ready datasets that power dashboards, copilots, and embedded analytics across the enterprise.

Key Responsibilities:


 Design, build, and maintain enterprise grade data models (facts, dimensions, and semantic layers) optimized for BI and GenAI consumption.
 Develop and manage certified data assets in Databricks and/or Microsoft Fabric OneLake, aligned to the Common Data Model (CDM).
 Implement metadata enrichment and governance using Databricks Unity Catalog, including business metadata, data classifications, ownership, lineage, and certification.
 Build GenBI applications using Databricks Genie, Microsoft Copilot and/or Fabric Data Agents, enabling natural language analytics, conversational BI, and insight generation.
 Hands on building SQL expressions within Databricks Genie to improve query processing, reduce latency.
 Creating benchmarks and instructions within Databricks Genie/ MSFT CoPilot to improve grounding and accuracy of Conversational AI models.
 Partnering with Cybersecurity to create right AD groups, access management and AD group mapping to Genies.
 Partner with data engineers to curate gold layer datasets (Delta tables / Lakehouse models) that are analytics and AI ready.
 Develop semantic definitions, KPIs, and metrics to ensure consistent enterprise reporting and AI interpretations.
 Enable self service analytics by publishing governed datasets and models for Power BI, Databricks SQL, and Copilot experiences.
 Apply data quality, validation, and observability checks to ensure accuracy, freshness, and reliability of certified datasets.
 Support responsible AI and data governance practices, including PII classification, access controls, and auditability.
 Document data products, models, and GenBI use cases, including business definitions and success metrics.



Qualifications:


 4–6 years of experience in data engineering, BI engineering, analytics engineering, or strong academic/portfolio projects.
 Strong SQL skills and proficiency in Python for data transformation and validation.
 Experience or exposure to data modeling concepts (star schema, snowflake, semantic models, metrics layers).
 Familiarity with Databricks (Delta Lake, Databricks SQL) and/or Microsoft Fabric (One Lake, Warehousing) and proven track record of building GenBI application/Conversational analytics leveraging Databricks Genie.
 Understanding of metadata, data governance, and cataloging concepts.
 Experience with Git based version control and CI/CD for data pipelines or analytics artifacts.
 Strong analytical thinking, problem solving, and ability to collaborate with business and technical stakeholders.



Preferred Experience:


 Hands on experience with Databricks Unity Catalog for data governance, lineage, and certification.
 Experience building and consuming Common Data Model (CDM) aligned datasets.
 Exposure to Databricks Genie, Microsoft Copilot, or conversational BI models and tools.
 Knowledge of BI tools such as Power BI or Databricks SQL dashboards.
 Familiarity with enterprise domains such as Finance, Procurement, HR, Supply Chain, or Manufacturing analytics.
 Understanding of data quality frameworks, KPI definitions, and metric governance.
 Relevant certifications (e.g., Databricks Data Engineer, Fabric Analytics Engineer, Power BI).



Success Metrics:


 Delivery of certified, well documented data assets adopted by BI and GenAI use cases.
 Demonstrated improvements in data trust, discoverability, and self service analytics adoption.
 Successful enablement of GenBI experiences with accurate, explainable, and governed results.
 Adherence to enterprise architecture, data governance, security, and responsible AI standards.

description:
About the job

Role: AI Data Collector (Remote)

Location: Work from anywhere

Job Type: Part-Time / Flexible

Daily Commitment: Flexible

No language barriers — this project records tasks only; no audio or speech required.

No experience required.




About the Role

Rilith AI is partnering with contributors — students, working professionals (office/field), and homemakers — to work on a research project in which participants record short egocentric (first-person POV) videos of everyday household tasks and daily activities performed at their work locations. You will record your hands carrying out real-life activities — at home or at work — with no extra time required beyond your normal routine. Everybody can participate.




Pay Rate

USD 10 per hour — United States
USD 6.5 per hour — Canada, Mexico, Brazil, Colombia, Argentina




Each participant can record up to 100 hours per environment (e.g. home, warehouse).




Eligibility Requirements

You must:

Reside in the United States, Canada, Mexico, Brazil, Colombia, or Argentina.
Own a compatible device:
Apple iPhone — iPhone 11 through 17 (all variants: Mini / Plus / Pro / Pro Max) (Not accepted: iPhone 16e, iPhone 17e)
Google Pixel — Pixel 6 through 10 (standard & Pro)
Samsung Galaxy — Galaxy S21 through S26 (standard / + / Ultra)




What You Will Do

Record first-person (egocentric) videos while performing real-life activities, including:

Household tasks (cooking, cleaning, laundry, and more)
Warehouse operations
Logistics activities
Retail and commercial operations

Check the app for the full list of available tasks.




Bonus & Payment Terms

Pay rate: USD 10/hr (US) / USD 6/hr (LATAM)
Payment method: PayPal
Flexible payment options - hourly, daily, weekly - as per the participants




Why Join?

Contribute to the development of next-generation AI and robotics systems
Get paid for every hour of video recorded in accordance with project guidelines
Start earning from day one
Record during your normal daily routine — no extra time needed
24/7 expert support available
Students and working professionals may request a certificate of contribution upon completion

Your data will remain secure. No personal information will be shared or disclosed without your consent.




About Rilith:

Rilith is empowering intelligent machines at scale. With teams spanning the USA, LATAM, EU, and Asia, we bring a truly global perspective to every project we undertake. Our on-the-ground experience extends beyond software and data — our team has hands-on experience working directly alongside robotic hardware, giving us a deeper understanding of real-world deployment needs. At Rilith, we take data responsibility seriously; all footage we collect is PII-free, with no face capture, ensuring every deliverable is fully privacy-safe. We don't just collect data — we collect it the right way.




We are highly agile and adaptive to challenging data projects.




Specializations:

Egocentric Head-Mounted Video – First-person POV captures using head-mounted rigs across residential and commercial settings. We handle participant recruitment, field deployments, and high-quality footage delivery.

Egocentric Human Hand Video – Hand-object interaction recordings in manufacturing, household, and daily-use settings using a multi-view rig (head, left-wrist, and right-wrist cameras).

Robotics Manipulation – Video capture of robotic gripper (2–3 finger) and dexterous hand (4–5 finger) tasks using a multi-view rig (head/overhead, left/right gripper, and front/top views).




Join our global network of Rilith Warriors:

description:
About the job

About Signify

Through bold discovery and cutting-edge innovation, we lead an industry that is vital for the future of our planet: lighting. Through our leadership in connected lighting and the Internet of Things, we're breaking new ground in data analytics, AI, and smart solutions for homes, offices, cities, and beyond.

At Signify, you can shape tomorrow by building on our incredible 125+ year legacy while working toward even bolder sustainability goals. Our culture of continuous learning, creativity, and commitment to diversity and inclusion empowers you to grow your skills and career.

Join us, and together, we’ll transform our industry, making a lasting difference for brighter lives and a better world.

More About The Role

We’re looking for an Intern to join our Consumer Channel team in Mexico.

Working for Signify means being creative and adaptive. Our culture of continuous learning and commitment to diversity and inclusion creates an environment that allows you to build your skills and career. Together, we’re transforming our industry.

As the world leader in lighting, we’re constantly ahead of the curve. Through our leadership in connected lighting and the Internet of Things, we’re breaking new grounds in data analytics, AI, and smart homes, offices, cities and more!

Signify is proud to be a leader in climate action, acting in line with the latest climate science. We are committed to reaching net zero greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions by 2040.

As an Intern / Apprentice you’ll be responsible for supporting Sales team and SOM role to improve customer service level in all the region (MEX & NOLA).

We’re on the lookout for forward-thinking innovators with a passion for sustainability. If you match this description, get in touch!

What You’ll Do


Sales Dashboard construction
Inventory level tracking (on hand / in transit) – Signify and Customer analysis
Slowmovers Action Tracker development
Sell In / Sell Out Consolidation ( 12 – 24 months)
Provisions report


Your Qualifications


Industrial Engineering or Business student (graduating after June 2027)
Intermediate English
Excel proficiency
Proactive


Our candidate profile is just a guide. We know that many talented people only apply for jobs when there is an exact match. If you don't fit all the criteria but believe this role is for you, we would very much like to hear from you.

We’re offering multiple opportunities for career development, like mentoring, coaching and stretch assignments, with strong international exposure for dynamic profiles. Learning is fundamental to our culture. Through continuous learning, we are transforming the industry. You have the chance to learn every day, acquire new skills and perspectives through customized online programs, and on-the-job experiences.

See #SignifyLife through the eyes of our employees!

Everything we’ll do for you

You can grow a lasting career here. We’ll encourage you, support you, and challenge you. We’ll help you learn and progress in a way that’s right for you, with coaching and mentoring along the way. We’ll listen to you too, because we see and value every one of our 27,000+ people. We believe that a diverse and inclusive workplace fosters creativity, innovation, and a full spectrum of bright ideas. With a global workforce present in 70+ countries, we are dedicated to creating an inclusive environment where every voice is heard and valued, helping us all achieve more together.

Come join us, and together we can light up the future.

title: Junior Media Buyer for a US Performance Marketing Agency (Remote)
skills: Building, launching, and managing Meta campaigns (CBO/ABO structures, audiences, placements, creative testing).
Supporting and growing into independent buying on AppLovin / Axon.
Monitoring daily performance and flagging scaling opportunities, underperformers, and anomalies.
Running structured creative and audience tests, and documenting the results.
Pulling, organizing, and interpreting performance data to inform decisions, and producing clear weekly client reports.
Analyzing creative performance using soft metrics (CTR, CPM, CPC), benchmarking against account averages, and clearly communicating those reads to strategists and clients.
Helping maintain clean account hygiene, naming conventions, and tracking.
0-2 years of hands-on paid media experience (internships, freelance, or running your own / your brand's ads all count).
Clear written communication in English.
Genuine fluency with Meta Ads Manager — you've built campaigns, not just read about them.
Strong numerical sense; comfortable living in spreadsheets and dashboards.
A direct-response mindset: you care about CPA, ROAS, and contribution margin, not vanity metrics.
Familiarity with DTC e-commerce, Shopify, and the fashion/beauty/lifestyle space.
Working knowledge of tracking and attribution tools (pixels, CAPI, ClickFlare, GA4, etc.).
Comfortable staying responsive when accounts demand it, including the occasional evening or weekend during launches or scaling pushes.
Availability during key EST windows to analyze performance and take action (scale, kill, pull back, etc.).
Experience with AdManage (strongly preferred).
Hands-on time with AppLovin / Axon or other programmatic / native platforms.
Basic understanding of creative.
Competitive base salary.
Fully remote, full-time contractor role.
Learn direct-response media buying directly from senior operators managing significant spend.
Real ownership early—you won't be stuck pulling reports for a year.
Work across a diverse roster of scaling, fast-growing DTC brands in fashion, beauty, and lifestyle.
A fast-paced team environment that rewards results and fosters growth into a senior media buyer role.
description:
About the job

Paired is a global staffing and recruiting agency that specializes in pairing remote work with top-tier talent!

We are dedicated to connecting talented individuals with remarkable companies in need of their skills. Our goal is to create fulfilling job opportunities for professionals around the world.

Our client:



Looking for a Junior Media Buyer who wants to learn the performance marketing craft properly. This is a hands-in-the-account role from day one for someone obsessed with what makes an ad account scale profitably, not just spend. You will work hand-in-hand with senior buyers to plan, launch, and optimize paid campaigns primarily on Meta, with growing responsibility on AppLovin / Axon.

You don't need years of experience, but you do need sharp instincts, comfort with numbers, and the drive to get great at this fast. You'll be building campaigns, reading data, spotting what's working, and learning to make confident scale/kill decisions across a portfolio of fast-growing DTC e-commerce brands.

This is a high-performance environment built for hungry, ambitious people who take real ownership of their accounts and treat client budgets like their own.

You'll be responsible for


Building, launching, and managing Meta campaigns (CBO/ABO structures, audiences, placements, creative testing).
Supporting and growing into independent buying on AppLovin / Axon.
Monitoring daily performance and flagging scaling opportunities, underperformers, and anomalies.
Running structured creative and audience tests, and documenting the results.
Pulling, organizing, and interpreting performance data to inform decisions, and producing clear weekly client reports.
Analyzing creative performance using soft metrics (CTR, CPM, CPC), benchmarking against account averages, and clearly communicating those reads to strategists and clients.
Helping maintain clean account hygiene, naming conventions, and tracking.


Requirements


0-2 years of hands-on paid media experience (internships, freelance, or running your own / your brand's ads all count).
Clear written communication in English.
Genuine fluency with Meta Ads Manager — you've built campaigns, not just read about them.
Strong numerical sense; comfortable living in spreadsheets and dashboards.
A direct-response mindset: you care about CPA, ROAS, and contribution margin, not vanity metrics.
Familiarity with DTC e-commerce, Shopify, and the fashion/beauty/lifestyle space.
Working knowledge of tracking and attribution tools (pixels, CAPI, ClickFlare, GA4, etc.).
Comfortable staying responsive when accounts demand it, including the occasional evening or weekend during launches or scaling pushes.
Availability during key EST windows to analyze performance and take action (scale, kill, pull back, etc.).
Nice to have


Experience with AdManage (strongly preferred).
Hands-on time with AppLovin / Axon or other programmatic / native platforms.
Basic understanding of creative.


Benefits


Competitive base salary.
Fully remote, full-time contractor role.
Learn direct-response media buying directly from senior operators managing significant spend.
Real ownership early—you won't be stuck pulling reports for a year.
Work across a diverse roster of scaling, fast-growing DTC brands in fashion, beauty, and lifestyle.
A fast-paced team environment that rewards results and fosters growth into a senior media buyer role.

description:
About the job

Job Title: QA Engineer (Remote)
Work Mode: Fully Remote
Schedule: Flexible (up to 40 hours per week, based on project availability)




Role Overview:

We are looking for software engineers across Latin America to join a paid remote AI training program. In this role, you will review and evaluate AI-generated code, rate AI responses for accuracy and quality, and write coding challenges to help improve AI model performance. This is a flexible, remote opportunity well-suited for software engineers seeking project-based, asynchronous work. No prior AI experience required.




Key Responsibilities

Review and evaluate code written by AI models for accuracy, efficiency, and best practices
Rate AI responses across key dimensions including correctness, instruction-following, and quality
Write original computer science questions and coding challenges to test AI capabilities
Identify bugs, errors, and inconsistencies in AI-generated code using structured guidelines
Provide clear, constructive, and structured written feedback to improve AI responses
Follow project guidelines, quality standards, and confidentiality requirements




Qualifications

Software engineering experience — junior, mid-level, or senior welcome
Proficiency in one or more of the following: Python, Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, C++, Swift, or Rust
Strong attention to detail, analytical thinking, and problem-solving skills
Background in full stack, backend, frontend, mobile, or any engineering discipline




Eligibility Requirements

Candidates must be legally authorised to work in one of the following locations:
Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Belize, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, Dominican Republic, Dominica, Ecuador, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama, Paraguay, Peru, Puerto Rico, Trinidad and Tobago, Uruguay, Venezuela




Compensation

Earn up to $34 USD/hr — core project rate with additional performance incentives averaging 7.5% on top
Payments issued weekly via supported payout platforms (e.g., PayPal or AirTM)




Work Arrangement

Fully remote and asynchronous
Flexible workload depending on project availability
Free access to leading AI tools worth $200+/month included




Equal Opportunity Statement Selection decisions are based solely on skills, qualifications, and project requirements. We are committed to inclusive and fair engagement practices and consider all qualified applicants without regard to legally protected characteristics.




Ready to put your coding skills to work on AI? Apply now — interviews begin within days of application.

About the job

Descripción Empresa

El Puerto de Liverpool es la empresa líder en tiendas departamentales en México. CON MÁS DE 170 AÑOS EN EL MERCADO, nos dedicamos a facilitar la selección de productos y brindarles el mejor servicio a las familias mexicanas, esperando superar las expectativas de calidad, moda, valor y atención dentro de un ambiente agradable.

Contamos con cinco unidades de negocio: Tiendas Liverpool, Tiendas Suburbia, Centros Comerciales Galerías, Boutiques y Negocios Financieros, componiendo una empresa sólida, que ofrece a sus colaboradores estabilidad financiera y el orgullo de pertenecer a una EMPRESA 100% MEXICANA.

NUESTRA MISIÓN ES SERVIR AL CLIENTE EN TODO LUGAR, TODOS LOS DÍAS, TODA LA VIDA, nuestra visión es ser la mejor opción en servicio, surtido y valor. Nuestros valores nos guían hacia el trabajo en equipo, productividad, innovación e integridad.

Misión Del Cargo

Gestion comercial de los diferentes clientes dentro de los Marketplace del país.

Funciones Del Cargo

Gestionar Mercado Libre, Amazon, Coppel y Marketplace de Liverpool.

Desarrollo de Portafolios de marcas.

The Coordinator will manage day-to-day project workflows, support partner communications, and maintain systems and processes that keep programs running smoothly.

The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced environment, is exceptionally detail-oriented, and is comfortable managing multiple projects while coordinating across internal teams and external partners.

Employment with Weave is contingent upon the successful completion of a background check, conducted in accordance with applicable laws.

At Weave, we use Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools to help us work more efficiently and create a smoother candidate experience. AI may assist with things like writing job descriptions, scheduling interviews, or reviewing applications against job-related criteria. For additional information, please review the External AI Policy Statement available on our Careers page.

Weave is an equal opportunity employer that is committed to fostering an inclusive workplace where all individuals are valued and supported. We welcome anyone who is hungry to learn, problem-solve and progress regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know.

Beware of recruitment fraud. All official correspondence will occur through Weave branded email. We will never ask you to share bank account information, cash a check from us, or purchase software or equipment as part of your interview or hiring process.

description:
About the job

About the Role

We are looking for a highly driven, entrepreneurial Business Development Representative (BDR) who thrives on prospecting, hunting, and uncovering opportunities others miss. This role is focused entirely on identifying high-potential prospects, generating interest through strategic outreach, and booking qualified meetings for our sales team.




Position - Remote




What You'll Do

Identify, research, and qualify prospective customers through multiple outbound channels
Build and maintain targeted prospect lists using a variety of tools, databases, and research methods
Analyze market signals, company developments, hiring trends, technology stacks, and other indicators to uncover sales opportunities
Execute personalized outreach campaigns via email, phone, LinkedIn, and other channels
Generate interest and schedule qualified meetings for Account Executives and sales leaders
Continuously refine prospecting strategies to improve conversion rates and pipeline generation
Maintain accurate prospect and activity records within the CRM
Collaborate with sales and marketing teams to optimize messaging, targeting, and outreach effectiveness
Proactively identify and build entirely new lead pools, industries, or market segments
Consistently meet and exceed activity, meeting, and pipeline generation goals




.What We're Looking for

Proven experience in Business Development, SDR, BDR, Lead Generation, or Outbound Sales
Strong prospecting and research skills with a hunter mentality
Passion for identifying ideal customer profiles, researching buyer behavior, and uncovering opportunities
Resourceful, self-directed, and capable of working with minimal supervision
Highly entrepreneurial with a strong sense of ownership and accountability
Comfortable operating in a fast-paced startup environment
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Tech-savvy and experienced with modern sales and prospecting tool
Naturally curious, analytical, and eager to learn
Ability to quickly adapt, execute proven processes, and contribute new ideas
Resilient, competitive, and motivated by achieving ambitious goals

About Monks

Monks is the global, digital-first, data-driven, unitary operating brand of S4 Capital plc. With a legacy of innovation and specialized expertise, Monks combines an extraordinary range of global Marketing and Technology Services to redefine how brands interact with the world. Through Monks.Flow, its flagship AI ecosystem for marketing orchestration, Monks transforms marketing into a growth engine, collapsing timelines and connecting brands to culture in real time. By deploying bespoke intelligent agents across disciplines and delivering culturally relevant, high-impact creative and digital solutions, Monks solves key critical business challenges across the entire brand enterprise to help brands sustain long-term impact.

Monks was named a Contender in The Forrester Wave™: Global Marketing Services, ranks among Cannes Lions' Top 10 Creative Companies (2022-25) and remains the only partner featured in AdExchanger’s Programmatic Power Players list every year (2020-24). Named Adweek’s first AI Agency of the Year (2023) and The One Show’s inaugural AI Pioneer Organization, Monks was also awarded Business Intelligence Group’s 2025 Excellence in Artificial Intelligence Award in both the Organizational and AI Product categories. As a trusted partner to cutting-edge innovators in tech, Monks earned titles such as Optimizely Experimentation Partner of the Year (2025), runner-up for the Adobe Firefly Partner Award (2024), and Workato’s AI Visionary Customer Impact Award (2024). Additionally, Monks achieved a record-breaking number of FWAs and continues to hold the most of any partner.

We are an equal-opportunity employer committed to building a respectful and empowering work environment for all people to freely express themselves amongst colleagues who embrace diversity in all respects. Including fresh voices and unique points of view in all aspects of our business not only creates an environment where we can all grow and thrive but also increases our potential to produce work that better represents—and resonates with—the world around us.

title: Associate Brand Manager
skills: Strategic Brand Planning and Insight Analysis : Actively drives the development, execution, and monitoring of annual brand plans while continually analyzing key performance indicators, industry trends, and competitor activities to deliver periodic brand reviews and actionable strategic recommendations.

Integrated Communication and Media Management : Coordinates end-to-end brand communication and media plans across all paid media, experiential activation, and public relations platforms, interfacing directly with agencies from initial creative briefing through to comprehensive final campaign evaluations.

Cross-Functional Commercial Collaboration : Serves as the primary strategic liaison between Sales, Customer Marketing, Global, and Regional teams to pitch marketing initiatives, facilitate key market visits, and uncover collaborative commercial growth opportunities.

Experiential Execution and Brand Advocacy : Leads total execution of brand experiential platforms and value-added packaging projects, maintaining strict brand guardianship across all channels to ensure the trademark is expressed consistently.

Financial and Administrative Performance Management : Manages the operational marketing budget, including comprehensive profit and loss monitoring, monthly advertising and promotion spend management, purchase order generation, and thorough invoice control.

Strategic Relationship and Agency Governance : Directs daily workflows and fosters robust partnerships with internal departments and external creative, PR, and media agencies to ensure all strategic communication output satisfies core business imperatives.

, : Controls assigned project life cycles from resource allocation to closure, leading robust post-evaluation reviews to implement key learnings that continuously optimize overall marketing efficiency.
Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or a closely related commercial field.
Professional Marketing Experience: Minimum 3 years of proven brand-building, portfolio development, and marketing success stories within fast-moving consumer goods global enterprises.

Analytical and Financial Competency: Strong numerical skills with a demonstrated ability to interpret Nielsen and ISCAM market research data, manage brand profit and loss structures, and optimize commercial performance metrics.

Strategic Communication and Language Skills: Intermediate professional proficiency in written and spoken English, paired with a demonstrated ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional global brand teams in multicultural environments.
Integrated Creative and Digital Mastery: Technical understanding of modern consumer digital marketing frameworks and contemporary multimedia production trends to drive innovative brand activations.
Experience: preferably in the Wine & Spirits, ready-to-drink (RTD), beer, or FMCG industries.
Project Management: Strong project management skills to handle multiple initiatives simultaneously.
Time Management: Ability to manage time effectively and meet deadlines.
description:
About the job

Quote From Hiring Manage r

The Associate Brand Manager will coordinate and execute the most important initiatives to reinforce the leadership of Jack Daniel´s, one of the upcoming portfolios in Mexico and the world.

Meaningful Work From Day One

The Associate Brand Manager coordinates the execution of comprehensive brand communication initiatives across paid media, experiential activation, and public relations platforms. This professional analyzes market dynamics, consumer behaviors, and competitor competencies to provide actionable insights that directly build brand performance, optimize portfolio value, and support strategic growth plans.

What You Can Expect


 Strategic Brand Planning and Insight Analysis : Actively drives the development, execution, and monitoring of annual brand plans while continually analyzing key performance indicators, industry trends, and competitor activities to deliver periodic brand reviews and actionable strategic recommendations.
 Integrated Communication and Media Management : Coordinates end-to-end brand communication and media plans across all paid media, experiential activation, and public relations platforms, interfacing directly with agencies from initial creative briefing through to comprehensive final campaign evaluations.
 Cross-Functional Commercial Collaboration : Serves as the primary strategic liaison between Sales, Customer Marketing, Global, and Regional teams to pitch marketing initiatives, facilitate key market visits, and uncover collaborative commercial growth opportunities.
 Experiential Execution and Brand Advocacy : Leads total execution of brand experiential platforms and value-added packaging projects, maintaining strict brand guardianship across all channels to ensure the trademark is expressed consistently.
 Financial and Administrative Performance Management : Manages the operational marketing budget, including comprehensive profit and loss monitoring, monthly advertising and promotion spend management, purchase order generation, and thorough invoice control.
 Strategic Relationship and Agency Governance : Directs daily workflows and fosters robust partnerships with internal departments and external creative, PR, and media agencies to ensure all strategic communication output satisfies core business imperatives.
 , : Controls assigned project life cycles from resource allocation to closure, leading robust post-evaluation reviews to implement key learnings that continuously optimize overall marketing efficiency.


What You Bring To The Table


Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or a closely related commercial field.
Professional Marketing Experience: Minimum 3 years of proven brand-building, portfolio development, and marketing success stories within fast-moving consumer goods global enterprises.
Analytical and Financial Competency: Strong numerical skills with a demonstrated ability to interpret Nielsen and ISCAM market research data, manage brand profit and loss structures, and optimize commercial performance metrics.
Strategic Communication and Language Skills: Intermediate professional proficiency in written and spoken English, paired with a demonstrated ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional global brand teams in multicultural environments.
Integrated Creative and Digital Mastery: Technical understanding of modern consumer digital marketing frameworks and contemporary multimedia production trends to drive innovative brand activations.


What Makes You Unique


Experience: preferably in the Wine & Spirits, ready-to-drink (RTD), beer, or FMCG industries.
Project Management: Strong project management skills to handle multiple initiatives simultaneously.
Time Management: Ability to manage time effectively and meet deadlines.


Who We Are

We believe great people build great brands. And we know there is Nothing Better in the Market than a career at Brown-Forman. Being a part of Brown-Forman means you will grow both personally and professionally. You will have the opportunity to solve problems, seize opportunities, and generate bold ideas. You will belong to a place where teamwork matters and where you are encouraged to bring your best self to work.

What We Offer

Total Rewards at Brown-Forman is designed to engage our people to ensure sustainable and profitable growth for generations to come. As a premium spirits company, we offer equitable pay structures for individual and company performance alongside a premium employee experience. We offer a range of premium benefits that reflect our company values and meet the needs of our diverse workforce.

description:
About the job

Overview

CAREERS TO SMILE ABOUT

At PepsiCo, you’ll discover a place where our mission is to create smiles around the world. With a portfolio of more than 500 beloved brands including, Gatorade, Lay’s and Quaker, our work touches millions of people every day.

At the heart of the company is a team of thinkers, creators, and problem-solvers who collaborate to innovate and turn ideas into action. Driven by innovation and a focus on creating joyful moments through food and drinks, our decisions are guided by consumer centricity, creating opportunities for our associates to do meaningful work and make a lasting impact in the communities we serve.

Whatever your role, you’ll be part of a global community that values your ideas and empowers you to make an impact, on your career and on the world around you.

Responsibilities

The Opportunity

The Content Designer is responsible for designing and executing full content projects, including social media graphics, banners, POS materials, internal communications, and presentation decks. They support the creative strategy and actively contribute ideas. Working closely with senior designers, they refine concepts and ensure that every design aligns with brand and project goals.

This role involves developing design and creative proposals, as well as adapting and versioning existing assets across PepsiCo’s portfolio to meet customer and consumer needs.

Our Content Designer is a specialist with expertise in retail, print, and digital materials. The ideal candidate is detail-oriented and highly proficient in design software, capable of creating graphics from scratch, editing and manipulating creative assets, and producing production-ready materials. They will also participate in brainstorming and brand strategy sessions, contributing to consumer programs, long-term campaigns, sizzle videos, POS, and 3D concepts.

Your Impact

As Mktg Analyst your responsibilities would consist of:


Work on the conception of creative visual ideas based on communication objectives.
Generate fresh, innovative POS, OOH materials, and multimedia graphics within brand and design standards to support all distribution channels, social media, and marketing initiatives.
Adapt and adjust existing creatives, digital toolkits, key visuals, and social media posts.
Build POS or KV designs and concepts when needed.
Determine the best way to visually represent a brand concept, focusing on graphic and motion design.
Act as a brand guardian, ensuring strict adherence to brand guidelines.
Take ownership of projects and work effectively both independently and collaboratively.
Detail-oriented, with the ability to prioritize, multitask, and stay organized while responding to last-minute requests and adapting to business changes.


High aptitude for learning, adaptability, and a strong sense of urgency to get things done.

Qualifications

Who Are We Looking For?

Education


Bachelor’s degree in a related field (Advertising, Graphic Design, Multimedia Design, Visual Arts, Motion Graphics, etc.).


Experience


4+ years of creative agency experience in design, 3D, packaging, or POS projects.
Proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite, Photoshop, Illustrator; and Blender, After Effects or Premiere is a plus.
Experience collaborating with creative teams in agency or in-house environments.
Fluency in English and Spanish is required for this role (strong English communication skills are essential).
Experience with CPG brands is a plus.
Excellent communication skills with both design and account teams.
Ability to work independently with minimal supervision.
Strong time management skills and the ability to meet deadlines.
Knowledge of PowerPoint and Microsoft Office.
Strong problem-solving and communication skills, with a willingness to learn and develop new design abilities.
A portfolio showcasing diverse digital, KV, and POS/packaging work, standout projects are a must.


If this is an opportunity that interests you, we encourage you to apply even if you do not meet 100% of the requirements.

What can you expect from us:

 


 Opportunities to learn and develop every day through a wide range of programs.

 Development programs according to Leadership skills.

 Learning experiences with internal and external providers.
 We love to celebrate success, which is why we have recognition programs for seniority, behavior, leadership, moments of life, among others.
 Financial wellness programs that will help you reach your goals in all stages of life.
 A flexibility program that will allow you to balance your personal and work life, adapting your working day to your lifestyle.
 And because your family is also important to us, they can also enjoy benefits such as our Wellness Line, thousands of Agreements and Discounts, Scholarship programs for your children, Aid Plans for different moments of life, among others.


 

We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. We respect and value diversity as a work force and innovation for the organization.

description:
About the job

BAT is evolving at pace into a global multi-category business. Our purpose is to create A Better Tomorrow™ by Building a Smokeless World. 

To achieve our ambition, we are looking for colleagues who are ready to join us on this journey! Tomorrow can’t wait, let’s shape it together! 

British American Tobacco México has an exciting opportunity for a Marketing Global Graduate in Monterrey

Role Summary

Our Global Graduate Program (GGP) is an entry-level opportunity designed for recent college graduates with up to 3 years of work experience who wish to fast track their career within a global organization, and have the possibility, after 18 months of successful completion of our program, to work as a Junior Manager in our organization. BAT is a market leader in over 55 countries with over 55,000 employees.

¿WHAT DOES THIS MEAN FOR YOU? - AN EXPERIENCE LIKE NO OTHER 

For the driven, passionate, and ambitious recent graduates, our Global Graduate Program is the first step towards bigger and better things. It’s a real job from day one with world-class training, international opportunities, and the chance to reach your full potential.

Become a Global Graduate in Marketing

Our Global Graduate Program aims to develop exceptional commercial & strategical business skills - fully understanding the dynamics of the market you are operating in, and how the BAT Marketing departments such as Brands, Trade Marketing & Development, Consumer Experience, Consumer Insights and Foresights, Customer Management work together to achieve outstanding business results.

You will work as part of our accelerated management program in different marketing rotations, starting your career journey at Trade Marketing & Distribution across Mexico and moving forward into different marketing subfunctions in Area Office Monterrey. These rotations will allow you to develop and present strategic business projects addressing real opportunities and creating action plans

Your Key Responsibilities Will Include


Act as the main point of contact for customers, managing orders, providing portfolio recommendations, and optimizing the overall sales process. 
Analyze market trends (Nielsen, Sell Out, Key Accounts) to generate insights and drive portfolio growth actions. 
Develop, monitor, and execute strategic projects for key clients, ensuring alignment with business objectives. 
Track account performance, KPIs, and market performance indicators, defining and implementing action plans. 
Manage demand planning, including NPI forecasts, cycle volumes, and ensuring forecast accuracy for new SKUs. 
Ensure product availability by monitoring inventory levels and coordinating supply chain and volume negotiations. 
Collaborate with cross-functional teams (TM&D, Brands, Marketing) to translate insights into execution plans and track results. 
Execute commercial and digital strategies, including trade engagement, e-commerce content, and support for multi-channel campaigns (social media, CRM, Paid Media, etc)


¿ What are we looking for? 


Bachelor´s degree in Finance, Finance and Accounting, Business Intelligence, Global Business, Strategy and Business Transformation, Data Science and Mathematics, Industrial and Systems Engineering, Digital Transformation in Business, Business Management and Administration, Economics, International Finance, Business Engineering Management, Marketing, or related field.
Recent graduates (graduation from 2024 to June 2026).
Up to 1 year of professional experience. Experience in Data Driven Projects.
Fluent in English (advanced proficiency in oral & writing).
Efficient use of MS Office tools (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, SharePoint, Teams).
Open to relocate globally - global mobility and national mobility.
Results-driven professional,data-driven, analytical and ambitious, with strong interpersonal, teamwork, and negotiation skills; proactive, adaptable, and self-motivated with the ability to work independently.
Have leadership skills demonstrated through either relevant professional experience or extra-curricular activities within prestigious companies, NGOs, student associations, etc.


¿What we offer you?


We offer a market leading annual performance bonus (subject to eligibility) 
Our range of benefits varies by country and includes diverse health plans, initiatives for work-life balance, transportation support, and a flexible holiday plan with additional incentives 
Your journey with us isn't limited by boundaries; it's propelled by your aspirations. Join us at BAT and become a part of an environment that thrives on internal advancement, where your career progression isn't just a statement – it's a reality we're eager to build together. Seize the opportunity and own your development; your next chapter starts here. 
You'll have access to online learning platforms and personalized growth programs to nurture your leadership skills 
We prioritize continuous improvement within a transformative environment, preparing for ongoing changes. 


¿WHY JOIN BAT?

We’re one of the few companies named as a Global Top Employer by the Top Employers Institute – certified in offering excellent employee conditions.

Collaboration, inclusion and partnership underpin everything we do here at BAT. We are looking forward to enabling every individual to thrive, regardless of gender, sexual orientation, marital or civil partnership status, gender reassignment, race, religion or belief, colour, nationality, ethnic or national origin, disability, age, skills, experience, education, socio-economic and professional background, veteran status, perspectives and thinking styles. We know that embracing talent from all backgrounds is what makes us stronger and best prepared to meet our business goals.

We see the career breaks as opportunities not obstacles. Through The Global Returners program, we support professionals looking to restart their careers after an extended absence from the workforce (e.g. time out caring for family, parental leave, national service, sabbatical and/or starting an own venture).

Come bring your difference and see what is possible for you at BAT. Learn more about our culture and our award-winning employee experience here.

If you require any reasonable adjustments or accommodations to help you perform at your best during the recruitment process, you are encouraged to notify us. We are fully committed to support you by making appropriate arrangements for you to demonstrate your full potential.

description:
About the job

About Role
As a Fullstory Analyst/Onboarding Specialist you are the primary guide for our clients as they begin their journey with Fullstory. You will lead them through a structured process designed to educate, enable, and empower their teams to maximize the platform's value. This is a technical training role.
Key Responsibilities

Guiding Fullstory onboarding customers throughout the onboarding project, from kick-off to final training session.
Coaching on installation, answering basic questions, sharing best practices, and providing high-quality, live-instructor training of the Fullstory platform.
Working with the clients to schedule and keep onboarding on track against agreed-upon timelines.
CX insights/ Data analysis work with guidance from management and leadership.
Continuous review of Fullstory features, seeking training (self or guided) as needed.
Staying up to date on industry trends, uses of AI in analytics, and other relevant topics to the job/company/industry.
Participating and managing the completion of tasks on time and efficiently.
Building and reviewing presentation materials for client meetings, keeping accurate and detailed records/notes of training sessions, and communicating professionally to clients and team members.

Key Requirements

Completion of 4-year college degree in Human Computer Interaction (HCI), UX Research, Digital Marketing, Digital Analytics, or other related field.
6+ months of web analytics experience using tools such as Google Analytics, Fullstory, and similar tools.
1+ year of digital UX-related (web design, UX design, user research, front-end development, or other similar) experience.
Highly analytical, numbers-oriented, and able to interpret statistical data at both the user and business levels.
Working knowledge of digital marketing and digital commerce, specifically traffic acquisition, e-commerce, and lead generation practices and related technologies.
Strong attention to detail, extremely organized, and capable of managing projects with limited supervision.
Knowledge of website design and UX best practices, and understanding of web development.
Effective, proactive, and efficient communication skills.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, dynamic environment

Preferred Qualifications: Extensive experience with the Fullstory platform, familiarity with basic qual/quant research, strong understanding of how websites are developed and updated, including HTML, CSS, JavaScript and related front-end technologies , and working knowledge of mobile app development and related frameworks.
Working Conditions

Work Environment: 100% Remote
Schedule: Part-time employment.

Why Join Us?

You will report directly to the COO, ensuring your strategies are heard, valued, and implemented without bureaucratic red tape. APPLY NOW!

description:
About the job

We are looking for software engineers across Latin America to join a paid remote AI training program. In this role, you will review and evaluate AI-generated code, rate AI responses for accuracy and quality, and write coding challenges to help improve AI model performance. This is a flexible, remote opportunity well-suited for software engineers seeking project-based, asynchronous work. No prior AI experience required.

Flexible workload depending on project availability

title: Marketing Specialist
skills: Executes and participates in general marketing functions that are more routine in nature, require smaller spend budgets and requires minimal contact with clients. Requires oversight with developing approach for more complex marketing functions which include higher thresholds of spend levels.

Participates in collaboration with business development, customers, product management, sales and other business partners in support of client-customized, content-rich marketing collateral, such as proposals, corporate communication, and other marketing related information for marketing functions more routine in nature. Will make recommendations regarding marketing process. Supports the marketing approach within strategy and budget.
Supports the development of strategic marketing plans for existing lines and participates in the interpretation of plans into “big ideas” to extend the company’s brand vision.
Supports the strategic direction, growth, and vision of assigned strategic growth objectives for the segment and manages the execution of programs. Has minimal client contact.
Supports the development of multiple pieces of promotional and advertising materials, such as press releases, social and digital media published on-line via web sites, twitter, etc.

Based on assigned strategic growth initiatives, builds and maintains existing relationships with internal contacts, external communications consultants and/or agencies. Participates in meetings with internal and/or external clients to understand their ongoing strategies and how relationships can be strengthened within the client’s organization.
Supports the development of product promotion and launch strategies.

Beginning to act as a marketing advocate, communications consultant, and/or liaison for business support units and/or operational units within the organization. Supports communications with leaders in those units on specific communications issues and strategic growth initiatives and coordinates appropriate marketing resources to achieve their functional goals.

Recommends measurements for return on investment (ROI) for marketing projects within the specific goals of building and delivering campaigns, such as internal and external communications, product launches, new business units, acquisitions, and RFP/RFI proposals within planned scope, schedule, cost and quality, and trade shows/events. Tracks costs against measurements.
Bachelor's Degree
Relevant Experience or Degree in: Digital Marketing , Advertising ,
Typically Minimum 3 Years Relevant Exp
Prefer marketing service or marketing agency experience. Prefer sales force and email automation experience. Prefer payments industry experience.

Skills / Knowledge - Having wide-ranging experience, uses professional concepts and company objectives to resolve moderately complex issues in creative and effective ways. Having ownership of a sub-function, account or matrix management responsibilities, applies knowledge to meet goals, maintain relationships, propose opportunities to expand the business, and lead matrix teams. Some barriers to entry exist at this level (e.g., dept./peer review).

Job Complexity - Works on complex issues where analysis of situations or data requires an in-depth evaluation of variable factors. Exercises judgment in selecting methods, techniques and evaluation criteria for obtaining results. Networks with key contacts outside own area of expertise. Builds on/Maintains external relationships of assigned accounts.
Supervision - Determines methods and procedures on new assignments and may coordinate activities of other personnel (Team Lead).
Marketing production execution - Broad marketing services and/or print/production
Communication skills - Including communication with internal or external audiences
description:

Participates in collaboration with business development, customers, product management, sales and other business partners in support of client-customized, content-rich marketing collateral, such as proposals, corporate communication, and other marketing related information for marketing functions more routine in nature. Recommends measurements for return on investment (ROI) for marketing projects within the specific goals of building and delivering campaigns, such as internal and external communications, product launches, new business units, acquisitions, and RFP/RFI proposals within planned scope, schedule, cost and quality, and trade shows/events. Minimum Qualifications


Bachelor's Degree
