title: Trafficker Digital / Especialista en Paid Media
description:
About the job

Sobre Backdoor Marketing

Somos una agencia de marketing digital que maneja múltiples cuentas. Buscamos un(a) Trafficker Digital que quiera crecer en pauta digital al lado de un equipo con experiencia.




IMPORTANTE — Esquema de colaboración:

Esta es una colaboración como PROFESIONAL INDEPENDIENTE (prestación de servicios profesionales), no una contratación como empleado interno. Tú emites factura (CFDI) por tus servicios. Buscamos personas que puedan facturar o estén dispuestas a darse de alta en el SAT; te orientamos en el proceso.




Responsabilidades:

· Creación, configuración, monitoreo y optimización de campañas en Google Ads y Meta Ads (también LinkedIn y TikTok) para 8 a 10 cuentas.

· Optimización diaria: presupuestos, pujas, públicos y rendimiento. Los creativos los entrega el Content Manager; tú te enfocas en performance.

· Medición y reportes: Google Analytics 4, Google Tag Manager, eventos y conversiones, con métricas clave (CPA, ROAS, CTR).

· Trabajo cercano con el equipo de contenido.




Requisitos:

· 1+ año en pauta digital (Google y Meta Ads), o certificaciones más práctica demostrable.

· Mentalidad analítica y orientación a resultados.

· Manejo de herramientas de IA/automatización para optimizar a escala.

· Autonomía y organización: trabajas por objetivos y entregables.

· Capacidad de emitir CFDI o disposición a darte de alta en RESICO.




Lo que ofrecemos:

· Colaboración como profesional independiente (tú facturas tus servicios).

· Mejor ingreso neto: bajo el régimen RESICO el ISR es de 1 a 2.5%, mucho menor que las retenciones de un sueldo.

· Esquema híbrido: trabajas principalmente desde casa, con visitas ocasionales a oficina o cliente.

· Libertad para manejar otros clientes.

· Pago mensual competitivo más bonos por desempeño.




Para postularte, comparte tu CV y, si tienes, casos o cuentas que hayas manejado.

title: Social Content Analyst
skills: Actuar como el radar del equipo mediante el monitoreo diario del entorno digital y la escucha activa de la comunidad y la competencia. Identificar de manera oportuna videos, formatos y temas virales en tendencia, traduciendo estos hallazgos en propuestas creativas e ideas accionables que permitan a la marca sumarse a la conversación digital de forma relevante y natural.

Contribuir al orden y la fluidez del flujo de trabajo diario y calendarios de publicación. El analista dará seguimiento a los tiempos de entrega entre los equipos de contenido, la agencia y los clientes internos para asegurar que los materiales estén listos en las fechas establecidas.

Enlace operativo para el equipo de Content Strategy, brindando asistencia directa en el armado de presentaciones, propuestas creativas y reportes. Tambien se encargará de documentar los acuerdos y minutas durante las sesiones de lluvia de ideas, dando un seguimiento puntual a las tareas para facilitar la comunicación interna y asegurar que la estrategia se traduzca de manera efectiva en la ejecución.

Extraer y recopilar las métricas de rendimiento de las piezas publicadas en los diferentes canales digitales. Validar que todo el contenido se haya implementado en tiempo y forma. Identificar formatos o publicaciones que hayan tenido un éxito sobresaliente para desarrollas mejores prácticas que sirvan como referencia y replicar en futuras producciones.
Licenciatura en Mercadotecnia, Ciencias de la Comunicación, Diseño Gráfico o afines.
Familiarización con plataformas sociales (Meta, X, TikTok)
Conceptos básicos de analítica digital y KPIs de redes sociales (Engagement, alcance, impresiones).

description:
About the job

¡Muchas gracias por tu interés en querer formar parte de nuestra familia #GenteViva!

En Viva nuestro principal propósito es que la gente se divierta, haga negocio y se conecte con sus seres queridos aprovechando la reducción de costo en el transporte aéreo.

¡Estamos seguros que con tu talento, seguiremos volando cada vez más alto!

Como Social Content Analyst serás responsable de contribuir a la fluidez operativa y logística del equipo de Content Marketing, que permita transformar el monitoreo de tendencias y la conversación digital en propuestas creativas que se puedan implementar, mientras se brinda soporte en la coordinación y ejecución de la estrategia de contenido.

Funciones Principales

Monitoreo de Tendencias y Creatividad


Actuar como el radar del equipo mediante el monitoreo diario del entorno digital y la escucha activa de la comunidad y la competencia. Identificar de manera oportuna videos, formatos y temas virales en tendencia, traduciendo estos hallazgos en propuestas creativas e ideas accionables que permitan a la marca sumarse a la conversación digital de forma relevante y natural.


Soporte Logístico y Operativo


Contribuir al orden y la fluidez del flujo de trabajo diario y calendarios de publicación. El analista dará seguimiento a los tiempos de entrega entre los equipos de contenido, la agencia y los clientes internos para asegurar que los materiales estén listos en las fechas establecidas.


Apoyo a la Estrategia y Coordinación


Enlace operativo para el equipo de Content Strategy, brindando asistencia directa en el armado de presentaciones, propuestas creativas y reportes. Tambien se encargará de documentar los acuerdos y minutas durante las sesiones de lluvia de ideas, dando un seguimiento puntual a las tareas para facilitar la comunicación interna y asegurar que la estrategia se traduzca de manera efectiva en la ejecución.


Análisis de Contenido


Extraer y recopilar las métricas de rendimiento de las piezas publicadas en los diferentes canales digitales. Validar que todo el contenido se haya implementado en tiempo y forma. Identificar formatos o publicaciones que hayan tenido un éxito sobresaliente para desarrollas mejores prácticas que sirvan como referencia y replicar en futuras producciones.


Habilidades y Conocimientos Técnicos


Licenciatura en Mercadotecnia, Ciencias de la Comunicación, Diseño Gráfico o afines.
Familiarización con plataformas sociales (Meta, X, TikTok)
Conceptos básicos de analítica digital y KPIs de redes sociales (Engagement, alcance, impresiones).
Nociones básicas de edición de video móvil o herramientas de diseño rápido (CapCut, Canva).
Creatividad.

description:
About the job

About Bedford

We're an email marketing agency for North American eComm brands. Our standard is 10/10 work, and we're scaling fast!




Role Description

You will own everything that brings amazing emails to life: the execution, the timelines, and the quality control. You will be the operational backbone of 10-15 brands at once. 




You will own:

Own the PM pipeline: manage our PM software to deliver emails on time. Chase approvals. Unblock blockers. Keep the weekly rhythm tight. Keep the team accountable.
Run the execution: build and maintain pop ups, flows, and campaigns.
QA everything: you're the last set of eyes before anything goes live. Links, segments, send times, etc. You check it.




You're a fit if:

Are relentlessly organized and fast
Have a sharp eye for mistakes — typos and broken links don't get past you
Follow up before anyone asks
Communicate clearly and directly
Have worked in eCommerce / DTC before

More details:

100% remote: but you must overlap with North American business hours
Paid in USD: reliably, every month
Long-term and stable: we want someone to grow with us for years, with real room to take on more as we scale

title: Junior Media Buyer for a US Performance Marketing Agency (Remote)
skills: Building, launching, and managing Meta campaigns (CBO/ABO structures, audiences, placements, creative testing).
Supporting and growing into independent buying on AppLovin / Axon.
Monitoring daily performance and flagging scaling opportunities, underperformers, and anomalies.
Running structured creative and audience tests, and documenting the results.
Pulling, organizing, and interpreting performance data to inform decisions, and producing clear weekly client reports.
Analyzing creative performance using soft metrics (CTR, CPM, CPC), benchmarking against account averages, and clearly communicating those reads to strategists and clients.
Helping maintain clean account hygiene, naming conventions, and tracking.
0-2 years of hands-on paid media experience (internships, freelance, or running your own / your brand's ads all count).
Clear written communication in English.
Genuine fluency with Meta Ads Manager — you've built campaigns, not just read about them.
Strong numerical sense; comfortable living in spreadsheets and dashboards.
A direct-response mindset: you care about CPA, ROAS, and contribution margin, not vanity metrics.
Familiarity with DTC e-commerce, Shopify, and the fashion/beauty/lifestyle space.
Working knowledge of tracking and attribution tools (pixels, CAPI, ClickFlare, GA4, etc.).
Comfortable staying responsive when accounts demand it, including the occasional evening or weekend during launches or scaling pushes.
Availability during key EST windows to analyze performance and take action (scale, kill, pull back, etc.).
Experience with AdManage (strongly preferred).
Hands-on time with AppLovin / Axon or other programmatic / native platforms.
Basic understanding of creative.
Competitive base salary.
Fully remote, full-time contractor role.
Learn direct-response media buying directly from senior operators managing significant spend.
Real ownership early—you won't be stuck pulling reports for a year.
Work across a diverse roster of scaling, fast-growing DTC brands in fashion, beauty, and lifestyle.
A fast-paced team environment that rewards results and fosters growth into a senior media buyer role.
description:
About the job

Paired is a global staffing and recruiting agency that specializes in pairing remote work with top-tier talent!

We are dedicated to connecting talented individuals with remarkable companies in need of their skills. Our goal is to create fulfilling job opportunities for professionals around the world.

Our client:



Looking for a Junior Media Buyer who wants to learn the performance marketing craft properly. This is a hands-in-the-account role from day one for someone obsessed with what makes an ad account scale profitably, not just spend. You will work hand-in-hand with senior buyers to plan, launch, and optimize paid campaigns primarily on Meta, with growing responsibility on AppLovin / Axon.

You don't need years of experience, but you do need sharp instincts, comfort with numbers, and the drive to get great at this fast. You'll be building campaigns, reading data, spotting what's working, and learning to make confident scale/kill decisions across a portfolio of fast-growing DTC e-commerce brands.

This is a high-performance environment built for hungry, ambitious people who take real ownership of their accounts and treat client budgets like their own.

You'll be responsible for


Building, launching, and managing Meta campaigns (CBO/ABO structures, audiences, placements, creative testing).
Supporting and growing into independent buying on AppLovin / Axon.
Monitoring daily performance and flagging scaling opportunities, underperformers, and anomalies.
Running structured creative and audience tests, and documenting the results.
Pulling, organizing, and interpreting performance data to inform decisions, and producing clear weekly client reports.
Analyzing creative performance using soft metrics (CTR, CPM, CPC), benchmarking against account averages, and clearly communicating those reads to strategists and clients.
Helping maintain clean account hygiene, naming conventions, and tracking.


Requirements


0-2 years of hands-on paid media experience (internships, freelance, or running your own / your brand's ads all count).
Clear written communication in English.
Genuine fluency with Meta Ads Manager — you've built campaigns, not just read about them.
Strong numerical sense; comfortable living in spreadsheets and dashboards.
A direct-response mindset: you care about CPA, ROAS, and contribution margin, not vanity metrics.
Familiarity with DTC e-commerce, Shopify, and the fashion/beauty/lifestyle space.
Working knowledge of tracking and attribution tools (pixels, CAPI, ClickFlare, GA4, etc.).
Comfortable staying responsive when accounts demand it, including the occasional evening or weekend during launches or scaling pushes.
Availability during key EST windows to analyze performance and take action (scale, kill, pull back, etc.).
Nice to have


Experience with AdManage (strongly preferred).
Hands-on time with AppLovin / Axon or other programmatic / native platforms.
Basic understanding of creative.


Benefits


Competitive base salary.
Fully remote, full-time contractor role.
Learn direct-response media buying directly from senior operators managing significant spend.
Real ownership early—you won't be stuck pulling reports for a year.
Work across a diverse roster of scaling, fast-growing DTC brands in fashion, beauty, and lifestyle.
A fast-paced team environment that rewards results and fosters growth into a senior media buyer role.

title: GenBI Engineer​
skills: Design, build, and maintain enterprise grade data models (facts, dimensions, and semantic layers) optimized for BI and GenAI consumption.
Develop and manage certified data assets in Databricks and/or Microsoft Fabric OneLake, aligned to the Common Data Model (CDM).
Implement metadata enrichment and governance using Databricks Unity Catalog, including business metadata, data classifications, ownership, lineage, and certification.
Build GenBI applications using Databricks Genie, Microsoft Copilot and/or Fabric Data Agents, enabling natural language analytics, conversational BI, and insight generation.
Hands on building SQL expressions within Databricks Genie to improve query processing, reduce latency.
Creating benchmarks and instructions within Databricks Genie/ MSFT CoPilot to improve grounding and accuracy of Conversational AI models.
Partnering with Cybersecurity to create right AD groups, access management and AD group mapping to Genies.
Partner with data engineers to curate gold layer datasets (Delta tables / Lakehouse models) that are analytics and AI ready.
Develop semantic definitions, KPIs, and metrics to ensure consistent enterprise reporting and AI interpretations.
Enable self service analytics by publishing governed datasets and models for Power BI, Databricks SQL, and Copilot experiences.
Apply data quality, validation, and observability checks to ensure accuracy, freshness, and reliability of certified datasets.
Support responsible AI and data governance practices, including PII classification, access controls, and auditability.
Document data products, models, and GenBI use cases, including business definitions and success metrics.
4–6 years of experience in data engineering, BI engineering, analytics engineering, or strong academic/portfolio projects.
Strong SQL skills and proficiency in Python for data transformation and validation.
Experience or exposure to data modeling concepts (star schema, snowflake, semantic models, metrics layers).

Familiarity with Databricks (Delta Lake, Databricks SQL) and/or Microsoft Fabric (One Lake, Warehousing) and proven track record of building GenBI application/Conversational analytics leveraging Databricks Genie.
Understanding of metadata, data governance, and cataloging concepts.
Experience with Git based version control and CI/CD for data pipelines or analytics artifacts.
Strong analytical thinking, problem solving, and ability to collaborate with business and technical stakeholders.
Hands on experience with Databricks Unity Catalog for data governance, lineage, and certification.
Experience building and consuming Common Data Model (CDM) aligned datasets.
Exposure to Databricks Genie, Microsoft Copilot, or conversational BI models and tools.
Knowledge of BI tools such as Power BI or Databricks SQL dashboards.
Familiarity with enterprise domains such as Finance, Procurement, HR, Supply Chain, or Manufacturing analytics.
Understanding of data quality frameworks, KPI definitions, and metric governance.
Relevant certifications (e.g., Databricks Data Engineer, Fabric Analytics Engineer, Power BI).
Delivery of certified, well documented data assets adopted by BI and GenAI use cases.
Demonstrated improvements in data trust, discoverability, and self service analytics adoption.
Successful enablement of GenBI experiences with accurate, explainable, and governed results.
Adherence to enterprise architecture, data governance, security, and responsible AI standards.
description:
About the job

GenBI Engineer (Generative Business Intelligence Engineer)

Location: CDMX / Remote

Kohler Opportunity

Role Summary:

Join Kohler’s AI Innovation team – Enterprise Architecture Office team to design and deliver Generative Business Intelligence (GenBI) / Conversational AI solutions that enable trusted, self service analytics and AI assisted insights. As a GenBI Engineer I, you will focus on enterprise data modeling, certified data assets, and metadata enrichment, while building conversational and GenAI powered BI experiences using Databricks Genie, Microsoft Copilot and/or Fabric Data Agents.

You will work extensively with Databricks, Unity Catalog, and/or Microsoft Fabric - One Lake, contributing to standardized semantic models aligned with the Common Data Model (CDM). The role emphasizes data trust, governance, discoverability, and AI ready datasets that power dashboards, copilots, and embedded analytics across the enterprise.

Key Responsibilities:


 Design, build, and maintain enterprise grade data models (facts, dimensions, and semantic layers) optimized for BI and GenAI consumption.
 Develop and manage certified data assets in Databricks and/or Microsoft Fabric OneLake, aligned to the Common Data Model (CDM).
 Implement metadata enrichment and governance using Databricks Unity Catalog, including business metadata, data classifications, ownership, lineage, and certification.
 Build GenBI applications using Databricks Genie, Microsoft Copilot and/or Fabric Data Agents, enabling natural language analytics, conversational BI, and insight generation.
 Hands on building SQL expressions within Databricks Genie to improve query processing, reduce latency.
 Creating benchmarks and instructions within Databricks Genie/ MSFT CoPilot to improve grounding and accuracy of Conversational AI models.
 Partnering with Cybersecurity to create right AD groups, access management and AD group mapping to Genies.
 Partner with data engineers to curate gold layer datasets (Delta tables / Lakehouse models) that are analytics and AI ready.
 Develop semantic definitions, KPIs, and metrics to ensure consistent enterprise reporting and AI interpretations.
 Enable self service analytics by publishing governed datasets and models for Power BI, Databricks SQL, and Copilot experiences.
 Apply data quality, validation, and observability checks to ensure accuracy, freshness, and reliability of certified datasets.
 Support responsible AI and data governance practices, including PII classification, access controls, and auditability.
 Document data products, models, and GenBI use cases, including business definitions and success metrics.



Qualifications:


 4–6 years of experience in data engineering, BI engineering, analytics engineering, or strong academic/portfolio projects.
 Strong SQL skills and proficiency in Python for data transformation and validation.
 Experience or exposure to data modeling concepts (star schema, snowflake, semantic models, metrics layers).
 Familiarity with Databricks (Delta Lake, Databricks SQL) and/or Microsoft Fabric (One Lake, Warehousing) and proven track record of building GenBI application/Conversational analytics leveraging Databricks Genie.
 Understanding of metadata, data governance, and cataloging concepts.
 Experience with Git based version control and CI/CD for data pipelines or analytics artifacts.
 Strong analytical thinking, problem solving, and ability to collaborate with business and technical stakeholders.



Preferred Experience:


 Hands on experience with Databricks Unity Catalog for data governance, lineage, and certification.
 Experience building and consuming Common Data Model (CDM) aligned datasets.
 Exposure to Databricks Genie, Microsoft Copilot, or conversational BI models and tools.
 Knowledge of BI tools such as Power BI or Databricks SQL dashboards.
 Familiarity with enterprise domains such as Finance, Procurement, HR, Supply Chain, or Manufacturing analytics.
 Understanding of data quality frameworks, KPI definitions, and metric governance.
 Relevant certifications (e.g., Databricks Data Engineer, Fabric Analytics Engineer, Power BI).



Success Metrics:


 Delivery of certified, well documented data assets adopted by BI and GenAI use cases.
 Demonstrated improvements in data trust, discoverability, and self service analytics adoption.
 Successful enablement of GenBI experiences with accurate, explainable, and governed results.
 Adherence to enterprise architecture, data governance, security, and responsible AI standards.



 Why Choose Kohler? 

We empower each associate to #BecomeMoreAtKohler with a competitive total rewards package to support your health and wellbeing, access to career growth and development opportunities, a diverse and inclusive workplace, and a strong culture of innovation. With more than 30,000 bold leaders across the globe, we’re driving meaningful change in our mission to help people live gracious, healthy, and sustainable lives.

About Us

If, as an individual with a disability, you need reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process, please contact kohlerjobs@kohler.com . Kohler Co. is an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer.

description:
About the job

Role: AI Data Collector (Remote)

Location: Work from anywhere

Job Type: Part-Time / Flexible

Daily Commitment: Flexible

No language barriers — this project records tasks only; no audio or speech required.

No experience required.




About the Role

Rilith AI is partnering with contributors — students, working professionals (office/field), and homemakers — to work on a research project in which participants record short egocentric (first-person POV) videos of everyday household tasks and daily activities performed at their work locations. You will record your hands carrying out real-life activities — at home or at work — with no extra time required beyond your normal routine. Everybody can participate.




Pay Rate

USD 10 per hour — United States
USD 6.5 per hour — Canada, Mexico, Brazil, Colombia, Argentina




Each participant can record up to 100 hours per environment (e.g. home, warehouse).




Eligibility Requirements

You must:

Reside in the United States, Canada, Mexico, Brazil, Colombia, or Argentina.
Own a compatible device:
Apple iPhone — iPhone 11 through 17 (all variants: Mini / Plus / Pro / Pro Max) (Not accepted: iPhone 16e, iPhone 17e)
Google Pixel — Pixel 6 through 10 (standard & Pro)
Samsung Galaxy — Galaxy S21 through S26 (standard / + / Ultra)




What You Will Do

Record first-person (egocentric) videos while performing real-life activities, including:

Household tasks (cooking, cleaning, laundry, and more)
Warehouse operations
Logistics activities
Retail and commercial operations

Check the app for the full list of available tasks.




Bonus & Payment Terms

Pay rate: USD 10/hr (US) / USD 6/hr (LATAM)
Payment method: PayPal
Flexible payment options - hourly, daily, weekly - as per the participants




Why Join?

Contribute to the development of next-generation AI and robotics systems
Get paid for every hour of video recorded in accordance with project guidelines
Start earning from day one
Record during your normal daily routine — no extra time needed
24/7 expert support available
Students and working professionals may request a certificate of contribution upon completion

Your data will remain secure. No personal information will be shared or disclosed without your consent.




About Rilith:

Rilith is empowering intelligent machines at scale. With teams spanning the USA, LATAM, EU, and Asia, we bring a truly global perspective to every project we undertake. Our on-the-ground experience extends beyond software and data — our team has hands-on experience working directly alongside robotic hardware, giving us a deeper understanding of real-world deployment needs. At Rilith, we take data responsibility seriously; all footage we collect is PII-free, with no face capture, ensuring every deliverable is fully privacy-safe. We don't just collect data — we collect it the right way.




We are highly agile and adaptive to challenging data projects.




Specializations:

Egocentric Head-Mounted Video – First-person POV captures using head-mounted rigs across residential and commercial settings. We handle participant recruitment, field deployments, and high-quality footage delivery.

Egocentric Human Hand Video – Hand-object interaction recordings in manufacturing, household, and daily-use settings using a multi-view rig (head, left-wrist, and right-wrist cameras).

Robotics Manipulation – Video capture of robotic gripper (2–3 finger) and dexterous hand (4–5 finger) tasks using a multi-view rig (head/overhead, left/right gripper, and front/top views).




Join our global network of Rilith Warriors:

Rilith has expertise in creating data specifically for Physical AI, which it does in collaboration with a globally spread freelancer network — we call them Rilith Warriors. What differentiates us from others is that we respect, care for, and understand the challenges faced by freelancers.

We ensure:

Timely payment
Continuous projects

Identificar de manera oportuna videos, formatos y temas virales en tendencia, traduciendo estos hallazgos en propuestas creativas e ideas accionables que permitan a la marca sumarse a la conversación digital de forma relevante y natural.

Contribuir al orden y la fluidez del flujo de trabajo diario y calendarios de publicación. El analista dará seguimiento a los tiempos de entrega entre los equipos de contenido, la agencia y los clientes internos para asegurar que los materiales estén listos en las fechas establecidas.

Enlace operativo para el equipo de Content Strategy, brindando asistencia directa en el armado de presentaciones, propuestas creativas y reportes. Tambien se encargará de documentar los acuerdos y minutas durante las sesiones de lluvia de ideas, dando un seguimiento puntual a las tareas para facilitar la comunicación interna y asegurar que la estrategia se traduzca de manera efectiva en la ejecución.

Extraer y recopilar las métricas de rendimiento de las piezas publicadas en los diferentes canales digitales. Validar que todo el contenido se haya implementado en tiempo y forma. description:
About the job

¡Muchas gracias por tu interés en querer formar parte de nuestra familia #GenteViva!

En Viva nuestro principal propósito es que la gente se divierta, haga negocio y se conecte con sus seres queridos aprovechando la reducción de costo en el transporte aéreo.

¡Estamos seguros que con tu talento, seguiremos volando cada vez más alto!

Como Social Content Analyst serás responsable de contribuir a la fluidez operativa y logística del equipo de Content Marketing, que permita transformar el monitoreo de tendencias y la conversación digital en propuestas creativas que se puedan implementar, mientras se brinda soporte en la coordinación y ejecución de la estrategia de contenido.

Funciones Principales

Monitoreo de Tendencias y Creatividad


Actuar como el radar del equipo mediante el monitoreo diario del entorno digital y la escucha activa de la comunidad y la competencia. Identificar de manera oportuna videos, formatos y temas virales en tendencia, traduciendo estos hallazgos en propuestas creativas e ideas accionables que permitan a la marca sumarse a la conversación digital de forma relevante y natural.


Soporte Logístico y Operativo


Contribuir al orden y la fluidez del flujo de trabajo diario y calendarios de publicación. El analista dará seguimiento a los tiempos de entrega entre los equipos de contenido, la agencia y los clientes internos para asegurar que los materiales estén listos en las fechas establecidas.


Apoyo a la Estrategia y Coordinación


Enlace operativo para el equipo de Content Strategy, brindando asistencia directa en el armado de presentaciones, propuestas creativas y reportes. Tambien se encargará de documentar los acuerdos y minutas durante las sesiones de lluvia de ideas, dando un seguimiento puntual a las tareas para facilitar la comunicación interna y asegurar que la estrategia se traduzca de manera efectiva en la ejecución.


Análisis de Contenido


Extraer y recopilar las métricas de rendimiento de las piezas publicadas en los diferentes canales digitales. Validar que todo el contenido se haya implementado en tiempo y forma. Identificar formatos o publicaciones que hayan tenido un éxito sobresaliente para desarrollas mejores prácticas que sirvan como referencia y replicar en futuras producciones.


Habilidades y Conocimientos Técnicos


Licenciatura en Mercadotecnia, Ciencias de la Comunicación, Diseño Gráfico o afines.
Familiarización con plataformas sociales (Meta, X, TikTok)
Conceptos básicos de analítica digital y KPIs de redes sociales (Engagement, alcance, impresiones).
Nociones básicas de edición de video móvil o herramientas de diseño rápido (CapCut, Canva).
Creatividad.
Microsoft Office (Word, PowerPoint, Excel).

title: B2B Marketing Consultant
description:
About the job

IMPORTANT - Watch this quick Loom video on how to get hired:

https://www.loom.com/share/345f776084e642ba99c24aabe40a67a0

C1 ENGLISH LEVEL IS REQUIRED, THE SELECTED CANDIDATE WILL BE SPEAKING STRICTLY IN ENGLISH DURING THEIR WHOLE SHIFT.

PLEASE SUBMIT YOUR RESUME AND AUDIO RECORDING IN ENGLISH




JOB TITLE

B2B Marketing Consultant

JOB ID

STASAM1R

INDUSTRY

Product Design and B2B Design Marketing

LOCATION

LATAM Only

JOB STATUS

Full Time

WORK SCHEDULE

8:30am to 5pm Monday to Friday EST (Flexible)

SALARY

1400 to 1800/month

TARGET START DATE

ASAP




Role Overview

The client is seeking a B2B Marketing Consultant to support the next stage of growth for its hospitality design licensing business.

This role is ideal for a strategic yet hands on marketer who can help connect brand storytelling, partner growth, sales rep enablement, customer insights, content execution, and marketing operations into one cohesive growth engine.

The consultant will work closely with leadership and the internal team to strengthen partner relationships, support licensed product collections, improve visibility into sales and specification opportunities, and build systems that help the business scale beyond founder led marketing.

This is not a traditional social media only or content only role. The right person should be able to move comfortably between strategy, execution, relationship management, project coordination, and commercial thinking.




About the Client

The client is a New York based design studio rooted in hospitality, known for creating trend forward licensed product collections across categories including flooring, wallcovering, textiles, tile, leather, furniture, and more.

The company partners with leading manufacturers to bring its designs into hospitality, commercial, and interior design projects around the world. The role of marketing is to strengthen the brand, support partner growth, increase product visibility, and create more opportunities for licensed collections to be specified in real projects.




Key Responsibilities

1. Partner Growth & Sales Enablement

• Support partner growth initiatives across manufacturers and licensed product categories.

• Help organize and maintain partner contact lists, sales rep directories, territories, and key relationship data.

• Coordinate with partner teams to improve visibility into sample activity, quote activity, project opportunities, and sales rep needs.

• Create or update sales enablement materials that help reps and partners present products more effectively.

• Support partner presentations, lunch and learns, CEUs, showroom activations, events, and co op marketing opportunities.

• Identify cross category opportunities where multiple product lines may be relevant to the same project.

2. Rep Ambassador Network

• Help launch, manage, and evolve the Rep Ambassador Network.

• Support the selection of pilot markets, partner approved reps, and ambassador candidates.

• Help coordinate workshops, rep feedback sessions, follow ups, and ongoing touchpoints.

• Maintain a simple tracking system for rep engagement, project intelligence, handoffs, sample/spec activity, and partner feedback.

• Support the development of ambassador tools, recognition ideas, badges, merchandise, and sales resources.

• Help turn field insights from reps into actionable marketing and partner growth opportunities.

3. Voice of Customer & Market Intelligence

• Conduct interviews with sales reps, interior designers, procurement contacts, and partner stakeholders.

• Capture insights around product feedback, specification behavior, market differences, sales objections, lost opportunities, and customer needs.

• Summarize findings into clear insights, recommendations, and action items.

• Help identify patterns by geography, product category, partner, designer segment, and project type.

• Use insights to improve content, product storytelling, sales tools, and partner support.

4. Marketing Strategy & 90 Day Planning

• Support the development of short term and long term B2B marketing priorities.

• Help define what initiatives should be prioritized, paused, simplified, or scaled.

• Create actionable plans that connect marketing activity to partner growth, sales enablement, brand awareness, and specification opportunities.

• Help the team stay focused on the most important growth levers rather than trying to execute too many disconnected initiatives at once.

5. Content, Social & Thought Leadership

• Support content planning and execution across LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, blog/editorial, newsletters, and partner channels.

• Help translate product collections, trends, and design concepts into clear, engaging, audience relevant storytelling.

• Draft captions, social posts, article summaries, email copy, partner updates, and thought leadership content.

• Coordinate with internal and external stakeholders to gather assets, organize content calendars, and support publishing workflows.

• Maintain a consistent, design forward, sophisticated, strategic, warm, and commercially relevant brand voice.

6. Lead Capture, CRM & Marketing Operations

• Support improvements to website lead capture, contact forms, inquiry routing, and follow up processes.

• Help organize and maintain contact databases, lead lists, partner lists, rep directories, and outreach trackers.

• Support light CRM or spreadsheet based tracking for leads, samples, quotes, reps, designers, and partner activity.

• Help create simple reporting structures that make it easier to understand what is working and where opportunities may be getting lost.

• Ensure marketing and sales related information is documented clearly so the team can act on it.

7. Events, Product Launches & Partner Activations

• Support planning and follow up for trade shows, partner events, showroom presentations, market visits, product launches, and hospitality design activations.

• Help prepare outreach emails, invite lists, event talking points, content capture plans, and post event follow up.

• Coordinate with partners to identify opportunities for collections to be featured in presentations, events, CEUs, lunch and learns, and client meetings.




Ideal Candidate Profile

The ideal candidate is a strong communicator, strategic thinker, and organized executor who understands how to connect marketing activity to business growth.

They should be comfortable working in a small entrepreneurial environment where priorities can shift quickly and where the ability to build structure is just as important as the ability to execute.




Required Strengths

• Strong B2B marketing experience

• Excellent writing and communication skills

• Experience supporting partner marketing, sales enablement, or business development initiatives

• Ability to manage multiple projects, timelines, stakeholders, and follow ups

• Strong organizational skills and comfort building trackers, lists, calendars, and simple operating systems

• Ability to turn conversations and insights into clear summaries, recommendations, and next steps

• Comfort working directly with founders, senior leaders, partners, sales reps, and creative teams

• Strong sense of brand voice, visual storytelling, and premium positioning

• Ability to work independently and proactively

Preferred Experience

• Experience in hospitality design, interiors, architecture, commercial design, lifestyle, luxury, or design adjacent industries

• Experience working with licensed product collections or manufacturer partners

• Familiarity with sales rep ecosystems, specification processes, design firms, procurement, or commercial interiors

• Experience with CRM, HubSpot, spreadsheets, contact management, or marketing operations

• Experience with Canva, social media scheduling, LinkedIn content, newsletters, or content calendars

• Experience conducting customer interviews or Voice of Customer research

Tools & Platforms

Experience with some of the following is helpful:

• Canva

• LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook

• HubSpot or similar CRM tools

• Google Sheets / Excel

• Google Drive / Dropbox / shared file systems

• Basecamp or project management tools

• Email marketing platforms

• Website CMS or form tools

• AI tools for writing, content support, research, and workflow efficiency

Success in This Role Looks Like

Within the first 30 to 60 days, the consultant should be able to:

• Understand the business model, partners, product categories, and core growth opportunities.

• Organize key partner, rep, designer, and project information into usable systems.

• Support the Rep Ambassador Network launch plan and workshop preparation.

• Improve visibility into sales rep activity, partner needs, and lead follow up.

• Create clear, polished content and partner communications.

• Help leadership prioritize the highest impact marketing and growth initiatives.

Within 90 days, success would include:

• A stronger partner and rep database.

• A clearer process for capturing and following up on leads, samples, quotes, and project signals.

• A launched or launch ready Rep Ambassador Network pilot.

• Consistent content and storytelling support.

• Stronger alignment between marketing, partner support, and sales enablement.

• Clear recommendations for what to scale next.

title: Remote Marketing Strategist
skills: Designs high-performance static and dynamic motion ads tailored specifically to the algorithms and specs of Meta, TikTok, and Google Display networks.
Produces short-form video content, kinetic typography, and animations (GIFs, motion graphics) designed to maximize engagement and watch time within paid channels.
Masters platform-specific technical requirements, striking a perfect balance between polished brand content and authentic, high-converting "UGC-style" aesthetics.
Architectures high-fidelity wireframes and designs for standalone landing pages, prioritizing Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO) and user experience (UX).
Creates visually engaging, responsive layouts for automated email lifecycle flows and editorial newsletters that maximize click-through rates (CTR).
Collaborates daily with the media buying team to audit performance data arrays, rapidly executing design iterations on "winning" assets to extend campaign longevity.
The "Conversion Eye": A profound understanding of visual hierarchy, direct-response design frameworks, and cognitive patterns that naturally guide a user’s eye toward a clear conversion event.
Multi-Disciplinary Toolbelt: Complete professional proficiency across Figma and the Adobe Creative Suite (specifically Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, and After Effects).
Speed & Operational Agility: A proven track record of maintaining exceptional aesthetic standards while operating comfortably at a high-velocity pace inside an agile marketing team.

Data-Informed Portfolio: A comprehensive design portfolio showcasing real-world marketing assets rather than abstract art. The candidate must be equipped to explain the analytical data, testing variables, and conversion "why" behind their design executions.

Workplace Infrastructure: Ownership of a high-performance computer setup capable of handling complex video rendering and multi-frame animation software, a dedicated home office workspace, and a stable, high-speed internet connection.
Practical experience working natively within landing page builders such as Webflow, Unbounce, or Instapage.
Foundational understanding of digital marketing frameworks, including multi-variable A/B testing principles and customer acquisition cost (CAC) goals.
Direct background supporting fast-scaling D2C, E-commerce, or B2G/B2B SaaS performance marketing structures.
Performance-Based Compensation Growth: A wide baseline salary band ($1,200 – $3,000 USD) that rewards top-tier technical capability, high qualifications, and exceptional asset delivery speeds.
Direct Growth Autonomy: Step out of rigid corporate design constraints into a performance-driven sandbox where your creative variations directly scale revenue.
Highly Collaborative Environment: Partner closely with data analysts and media buyers, gaining elite, real-time feedback loops on your design executions.
100% Remote Infrastructure: Long-term, secure full-time contractor engagement managed entirely from the comfort of a dedicated home office.
description:
About the job

HireLatam is a premier recruitment agency that places top Latin American talent in independent contractor roles in US companies. With a proven track record and a commitment to excellence, we're your trusted partner in the pursuit of career success. Our extensive network, personalized approach, and supportive guidance ensure that you're in the best hands to find your next job opportunity.




Job Title: Remote Marketing Strategist (100% Work From Home) 

Location: Remote from Latin America

Position Type: Full-time

Salary: $1,200 – $3,000 USD/month (Fully dependent on creative skill level, qualifications, and design output speed)

Schedule: Monday – Friday, 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM US Eastern Time (EST), featuring a structured 1-hour lunch break from 12:00 PM – 1:00 PM EST.




About our Client




Our Client is a fast-growing, data-driven enterprise that relies on cutting-edge digital acquisition to scale its market presence. Operating with a high-velocity, performance-first marketing team, they prioritize conversion metrics over vanity metrics. They foster an agile, collaborative remote environment where creative assets are constantly measured, iterated upon, and optimized to drive real business growth and pipeline value.




The Role




Our Client is seeking a high-output, conversion-oriented Performance Designer to take complete ownership of visual assets across the entire customer acquisition funnel. This is not a traditional branding role focused solely on aesthetics; it is a high-ownership position where design must directly drive performance. The successful candidate will blend creative mastery with analytical reasoning to engineer thumb-stopping social ads, high-converting landing page layouts, engaging email campaigns, and short-form motion graphics that systematically guide users toward a definitive Call to Action (CTA).




Key Responsibilities




Paid Media Ad Creative & Motion Design




Designs high-performance static and dynamic motion ads tailored specifically to the algorithms and specs of Meta, TikTok, and Google Display networks.
Produces short-form video content, kinetic typography, and animations (GIFs, motion graphics) designed to maximize engagement and watch time within paid channels.
Masters platform-specific technical requirements, striking a perfect balance between polished brand content and authentic, high-converting "UGC-style" aesthetics.




Web Layouts, Lifecycle Marketing & Iterative Testing




Architectures high-fidelity wireframes and designs for standalone landing pages, prioritizing Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO) and user experience (UX).
Creates visually engaging, responsive layouts for automated email lifecycle flows and editorial newsletters that maximize click-through rates (CTR).
Collaborates daily with the media buying team to audit performance data arrays, rapidly executing design iterations on "winning" assets to extend campaign longevity.







Qualifications, Skills, and Key Competencies




Required Qualifications




The "Conversion Eye": A profound understanding of visual hierarchy, direct-response design frameworks, and cognitive patterns that naturally guide a user’s eye toward a clear conversion event.
Multi-Disciplinary Toolbelt: Complete professional proficiency across Figma and the Adobe Creative Suite (specifically Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, and After Effects).
Speed & Operational Agility: A proven track record of maintaining exceptional aesthetic standards while operating comfortably at a high-velocity pace inside an agile marketing team.
Data-Informed Portfolio: A comprehensive design portfolio showcasing real-world marketing assets rather than abstract art. The candidate must be equipped to explain the analytical data, testing variables, and conversion "why" behind their design executions.
Workplace Infrastructure: Ownership of a high-performance computer setup capable of handling complex video rendering and multi-frame animation software, a dedicated home office workspace, and a stable, high-speed internet connection.




Preferred Qualifications




Practical experience working natively within landing page builders such as Webflow, Unbounce, or Instapage.
Foundational understanding of digital marketing frameworks, including multi-variable A/B testing principles and customer acquisition cost (CAC) goals.
Direct background supporting fast-scaling D2C, E-commerce, or B2G/B2B SaaS performance marketing structures.




Benefits




Performance-Based Compensation Growth: A wide baseline salary band ($1,200 – $3,000 USD) that rewards top-tier technical capability, high qualifications, and exceptional asset delivery speeds.
Direct Growth Autonomy: Step out of rigid corporate design constraints into a performance-driven sandbox where your creative variations directly scale revenue.
Highly Collaborative Environment: Partner closely with data analysts and media buyers, gaining elite, real-time feedback loops on your design executions.
100% Remote Infrastructure: Long-term, secure full-time contractor engagement managed entirely from the comfort of a dedicated home office.

title: Influencer & Branded Content Coordinator
skills: Support the execution of influencer marketing campaigns from start to finish, helping to deliver outstanding, creative work for global brands.
Serve as a reliable day-to-day contact for clients—managing updates, timelines, and deliverables to ensure smooth communication and collaboration.
Partner with Account Managers and Directors to develop campaign briefs, creator contracts, and proposals, ensuring clarity and accuracy.
Contribute to pitches and presentations, helping bring creative ideas to life through data, insights, and storytelling.
Collaborate across influencer, creative, and strategy teams to deliver cohesive, effective client campaigns.
Track campaign performance and analyse data to identify key insights and learnings.
Maintain organized campaign folders, documentation, and reports, ensuring all information is accurate and easy to access.
Stay current on creator trends, platform updates, and brand partnerships, identifying new opportunities to elevate client work.
Monitor budgets, timelines, and performance targets to support the achievement of campaign and revenue goals.
Demonstrate curiosity and initiative—actively seeking opportunities to improve processes and contribute to the team’s success.
1-2+ years of experience in Influencer & Branded Content, with a bachelor’s degree or equivalent work experience.
Understanding of content creation and influencer marketing strategy, supporting campaign execution and brand partnerships.
Strong attention to detail orientation, ensuring accuracy and consistency in influencer and branded content initiatives.
Experience in project implementation, helping coordinate and manage influencer collaborations.
Basic proficiency in presentation skills, effectively communicating campaign results and insights.
Familiarity with advertising standards, client service, and relationship management, ensuring seamless influencer partnerships.
Exposure to influencer marketing dashboards and social media platforms, optimizing campaign tracking and performance analysis.
Experience in networking and influencer management, fostering strong industry relationships for impactful collaborations.

A commitment to curiosity and adaptability as a Continual Learner, staying at the forefront of media innovation while prioritizing client success with a Customer-Focused mindset that seeks opportunities to deliver meaningful value.
A dedication to People-Focused leadership, fostering collaboration, innovation, and professional growth by Leading by Example and nurturing strong relationships that empower others to excel.
Professional Development: Take advantage of our learning and development programs, mentorship opportunities, and career advancement support.
Generous Time Off: Enjoy generous paid time off and holiday allowances to recharge and spend time with loved ones.
Parental Leave: We provide paid parental leave to support your family during important life events.
Retirement & Pension Plans: Plan for your future with competitive retirement or pension programs, including contribution matching.
Fertility and Family Support: Access fertility benefits for all team members and their spouses.
Healthcare: Coverage and support for everyday medical expenses and routine care, tailored by geography.
Pet Insurance: Protect your pet's health and your finances.
Lifestyle Spending Accounts: Enjoy 100% company-funded accounts to promote healthy habits and well-being.
Commuter Benefits: Access support for travel and commuting needs, where available.
Annual Bonus: All employees are eligible for an annual bonus.
Volunteering Opportunities: Receive 8 give-back hours to volunteer in your local communities.
AI Enterprise License: Access AI Enterprise accounts and participate in weekly AI training sessions to empower and ensure AI safety.
description:
About the job

We are PMG

Named Ad Age Best Places to Work for 11 years in a row and Fast Company Best Workplaces for Innovators since 2022, PMG is a global independent marketing services and technology company that seeks to inspire people and brands that anything is possible. Driven by shared success, PMG leverages business strategy and transformation, creative, media, and insights—as well as its proprietary operating system, Alli—to deliver cutting-edge digital solutions and innovative media strategies.

Our team comprises over 900 employees globally, and our work for ambitious brands like Apple, Nike, Sephora, Experian, Therabody, Best Western Hotels & Resorts, Creed Fragrances, BNY, Intuit, CKE Restaurants, Travelex, and Whole Foods spans 85+ countries.

Professional proficiency in written and spoken English is required, including for resume submission and interviews.

Who You Will Be Working With

At PMG, our Influencer & Branded Content team is at the intersection of culture, storytelling, and strategy, helping brands connect with audiences in authentic, meaningful ways. We craft high-impact influencer campaigns that go beyond traditional advertising - driving engagement, building brand affinity, and delivering measurable results across social, digital, and experiential platforms.

You’ll collaborate with creators, talent agencies, media strategists, content teams, and brand partners to develop and execute influencer marketing programs that seamlessly align storytelling with business objectives. Whether it’s identifying the right talent, negotiating partnerships, overseeing content production, or optimizing campaign performance, your role will be instrumental in shaping how brands foster meaningful connections with their audiences.

Beyond execution, you’ll play a role in exploring new content formats, leveraging emerging platforms, and staying ahead of industry trends to ensure influencer strategies are not only relevant but also innovative and impactful. At PMG, we don’t just follow trends - we create them.

As an Influencer & Branded Content (title level), if you’re passionate about shaping culture through creator-driven storytelling, using data to drive real impact, and redefining what’s possible in influencer marketing, this is the place to make your mark.

What You Will Be Doing


Support the execution of influencer marketing campaigns from start to finish, helping to deliver outstanding, creative work for global brands. 
Serve as a reliable day-to-day contact for clients—managing updates, timelines, and deliverables to ensure smooth communication and collaboration. 
Partner with Account Managers and Directors to develop campaign briefs, creator contracts, and proposals, ensuring clarity and accuracy. 
Contribute to pitches and presentations, helping bring creative ideas to life through data, insights, and storytelling. 
Collaborate across influencer, creative, and strategy teams to deliver cohesive, effective client campaigns. 
Track campaign performance and analyse data to identify key insights and learnings. 
Maintain organized campaign folders, documentation, and reports, ensuring all information is accurate and easy to access. 
Stay current on creator trends, platform updates, and brand partnerships, identifying new opportunities to elevate client work. 
Monitor budgets, timelines, and performance targets to support the achievement of campaign and revenue goals. 
Demonstrate curiosity and initiative—actively seeking opportunities to improve processes and contribute to the team’s success. 


What You Will Bring


1-2+ years of experience in Influencer & Branded Content, with a bachelor’s degree or equivalent work experience. 
Understanding of content creation and influencer marketing strategy, supporting campaign execution and brand partnerships. 
Strong attention to detail orientation, ensuring accuracy and consistency in influencer and branded content initiatives. 
Experience in project implementation, helping coordinate and manage influencer collaborations. 
Basic proficiency in presentation skills, effectively communicating campaign results and insights. 
Familiarity with advertising standards, client service, and relationship management, ensuring seamless influencer partnerships. 
Exposure to influencer marketing dashboards and social media platforms, optimizing campaign tracking and performance analysis. 
Experience in networking and influencer management, fostering strong industry relationships for impactful collaborations. 
A commitment to curiosity and adaptability as a Continual Learner, staying at the forefront of media innovation while prioritizing client success with a Customer-Focused mindset that seeks opportunities to deliver meaningful value. 
A dedication to People-Focused leadership, fostering collaboration, innovation, and professional growth by Leading by Example and nurturing strong relationships that empower others to excel. 


What We Offer


Professional Development: Take advantage of our learning and development programs, mentorship opportunities, and career advancement support. 
Generous Time Off: Enjoy generous paid time off and holiday allowances to recharge and spend time with loved ones. 
Parental Leave: We provide paid parental leave to support your family during important life events. 
Retirement & Pension Plans: Plan for your future with competitive retirement or pension programs, including contribution matching. 
Fertility and Family Support: Access fertility benefits for all team members and their spouses. 
Healthcare: Coverage and support for everyday medical expenses and routine care, tailored by geography. 
Pet Insurance: Protect your pet's health and your finances. 
Lifestyle Spending Accounts: Enjoy 100% company-funded accounts to promote healthy habits and well-being. 
Commuter Benefits: Access support for travel and commuting needs, where available. 
Annual Bonus: All employees are eligible for an annual bonus. 
Volunteering Opportunities: Receive 8 give-back hours to volunteer in your local communities. 
AI Enterprise License: Access AI Enterprise accounts and participate in weekly AI training sessions to empower and ensure AI safety. 


Benefits vary by country, location, and geography to reflect local laws, norms, and expectations.

What Sets Us Apart

Being part of PMG means joining a company culture that’s unmatched in digital. We're dedicated to working hard to serve our employees and clients, delivering value, results, and innovation—which often requires true grit and agility. We believe in taking care of ourselves and each other to continuously improve in every way.

In alignment with our core values to be inclusive and always change for the better, PMG is committed to creating a more diverse and inclusive culture, and we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We believe that we only change for the better by bringing diverse perspectives to our company. PMG recruits, employs, trains, compensates, and promotes regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

title: Associate Brand Manager
skills: Strategic Brand Planning and Insight Analysis : Actively drives the development, execution, and monitoring of annual brand plans while continually analyzing key performance indicators, industry trends, and competitor activities to deliver periodic brand reviews and actionable strategic recommendations.

Integrated Communication and Media Management : Coordinates end-to-end brand communication and media plans across all paid media, experiential activation, and public relations platforms, interfacing directly with agencies from initial creative briefing through to comprehensive final campaign evaluations.

Cross-Functional Commercial Collaboration : Serves as the primary strategic liaison between Sales, Customer Marketing, Global, and Regional teams to pitch marketing initiatives, facilitate key market visits, and uncover collaborative commercial growth opportunities.

Experiential Execution and Brand Advocacy : Leads total execution of brand experiential platforms and value-added packaging projects, maintaining strict brand guardianship across all channels to ensure the trademark is expressed consistently.

Financial and Administrative Performance Management : Manages the operational marketing budget, including comprehensive profit and loss monitoring, monthly advertising and promotion spend management, purchase order generation, and thorough invoice control.

Strategic Relationship and Agency Governance : Directs daily workflows and fosters robust partnerships with internal departments and external creative, PR, and media agencies to ensure all strategic communication output satisfies core business imperatives.

description:
About the job

Quote From Hiring Manage r

The Associate Brand Manager will coordinate and execute the most important initiatives to reinforce the leadership of Jack Daniel´s, one of the upcoming portfolios in Mexico and the world.

Meaningful Work From Day One

The Associate Brand Manager coordinates the execution of comprehensive brand communication initiatives across paid media, experiential activation, and public relations platforms. This professional analyzes market dynamics, consumer behaviors, and competitor competencies to provide actionable insights that directly build brand performance, optimize portfolio value, and support strategic growth plans.

What You Can Expect


 Strategic Brand Planning and Insight Analysis : Actively drives the development, execution, and monitoring of annual brand plans while continually analyzing key performance indicators, industry trends, and competitor activities to deliver periodic brand reviews and actionable strategic recommendations.
 Integrated Communication and Media Management : Coordinates end-to-end brand communication and media plans across all paid media, experiential activation, and public relations platforms, interfacing directly with agencies from initial creative briefing through to comprehensive final campaign evaluations.
 Cross-Functional Commercial Collaboration : Serves as the primary strategic liaison between Sales, Customer Marketing, Global, and Regional teams to pitch marketing initiatives, facilitate key market visits, and uncover collaborative commercial growth opportunities.
 Experiential Execution and Brand Advocacy : Leads total execution of brand experiential platforms and value-added packaging projects, maintaining strict brand guardianship across all channels to ensure the trademark is expressed consistently.
 Financial and Administrative Performance Management : Manages the operational marketing budget, including comprehensive profit and loss monitoring, monthly advertising and promotion spend management, purchase order generation, and thorough invoice control.
 Strategic Relationship and Agency Governance : Directs daily workflows and fosters robust partnerships with internal departments and external creative, PR, and media agencies to ensure all strategic communication output satisfies core business imperatives.
 , : Controls assigned project life cycles from resource allocation to closure, leading robust post-evaluation reviews to implement key learnings that continuously optimize overall marketing efficiency.


What You Bring To The Table


Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or a closely related commercial field.
Professional Marketing Experience: Minimum 3 years of proven brand-building, portfolio development, and marketing success stories within fast-moving consumer goods global enterprises.
Analytical and Financial Competency: Strong numerical skills with a demonstrated ability to interpret Nielsen and ISCAM market research data, manage brand profit and loss structures, and optimize commercial performance metrics.
Strategic Communication and Language Skills: Intermediate professional proficiency in written and spoken English, paired with a demonstrated ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional global brand teams in multicultural environments.
Integrated Creative and Digital Mastery: Technical understanding of modern consumer digital marketing frameworks and contemporary multimedia production trends to drive innovative brand activations.


What Makes You Unique


Experience: preferably in the Wine & Spirits, ready-to-drink (RTD), beer, or FMCG industries.
Project Management: Strong project management skills to handle multiple initiatives simultaneously.
Time Management: Ability to manage time effectively and meet deadlines.


Who We Are

We believe great people build great brands. And we know there is Nothing Better in the Market than a career at Brown-Forman. Being a part of Brown-Forman means you will grow both personally and professionally. You will have the opportunity to solve problems, seize opportunities, and generate bold ideas. You will belong to a place where teamwork matters and where you are encouraged to bring your best self to work.

What We Offer

Total Rewards at Brown-Forman is designed to engage our people to ensure sustainable and profitable growth for generations to come. As a premium spirits company, we offer equitable pay structures for individual and company performance alongside a premium employee experience. We offer a range of premium benefits that reflect our company values and meet the needs of our diverse workforce.

title: Performance Marketing Specialist (Part-Time)
skills: Plan, manage, and optimize paid media campaigns across Meta Ads and Google Ads to drive qualified lead generation and improve ROI.
Execute ongoing campaign testing across audiences, creatives, bidding strategies, and landing pages to continuously improve performance.
Monitor and analyze campaign performance data, translating insights into actionable recommendations for optimization and growth.

Support SEO initiatives by contributing to on-page optimization and identifying opportunities for organic growth improvements.
Develop and deliver monthly performance reports, highlighting key metrics, insights, and optimization opportunities.
Identify trends, competitor movements, and emerging opportunities in digital marketing to inform campaign strategy.
Collaborate with stakeholders to improve messaging, creative direction, and conversion-focused strategies across campaigns.
Continuously optimize conversion rates and campaign efficiency across all digital channels.

Experience working with CRM platforms, marketing automation tools, and lead generation workflows.

Strong communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively with internal and external stakeholders.

Flexible work-life balance with a structured part-time schedule.
Opportunities for ongoing learning, professional development, and skill growth.

Employee engagement activities, both virtual and in-person events.

description:
About the job

This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Performance Marketing Specialist (Part-Time) based in Mexico.

This is a hands-on, data-driven digital marketing role focused on driving measurable growth across paid media channels. You will be responsible for optimizing performance campaigns across Meta and Google Ads, turning insights into actionable improvements that directly impact lead generation and conversion outcomes. The role blends campaign execution, analytics, and experimentation, requiring a strong focus on ROI, attribution accuracy, and continuous optimization. You will also support tracking infrastructure using tools such as GA4 and Google Tag Manager to ensure clean, reliable marketing data. Working in a remote-first and collaborative environment, you will contribute to both strategic and tactical marketing decisions. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys solving performance puzzles, testing ideas quickly, and seeing clear business impact from their work. It offers the opportunity to work across global clients while shaping scalable digital growth strategies.

Accountabilities


Plan, manage, and optimize paid media campaigns across Meta Ads and Google Ads to drive qualified lead generation and improve ROI.
Execute ongoing campaign testing across audiences, creatives, bidding strategies, and landing pages to continuously improve performance.
Monitor and analyze campaign performance data, translating insights into actionable recommendations for optimization and growth.
Maintain accurate tracking and attribution systems using tools such as GA4, Google Tag Manager, Meta Pixel, and related conversion tracking platforms.
Ensure seamless flow of marketing data into CRM and marketing automation systems for accurate reporting and lead management.
Support SEO initiatives by contributing to on-page optimization and identifying opportunities for organic growth improvements.
Develop and deliver monthly performance reports, highlighting key metrics, insights, and optimization opportunities.
Identify trends, competitor movements, and emerging opportunities in digital marketing to inform campaign strategy.
Collaborate with stakeholders to improve messaging, creative direction, and conversion-focused strategies across campaigns.
Continuously optimize conversion rates and campaign efficiency across all digital channels.


Requirements


Minimum 2+ years of hands-on experience managing and optimizing paid media campaigns.
Strong expertise in Meta Ads Manager and Google Ads, with proven ability to drive performance outcomes.
Solid working knowledge of Google Tag Manager (GTM), Google Analytics 4 (GA4), Meta Pixel, and conversion tracking systems.
Experience working with CRM platforms, marketing automation tools, and lead generation workflows.
Strong understanding of performance marketing fundamentals including attribution, funnel optimization, and ROI measurement.
Ability to analyze campaign data and translate insights into clear, actionable recommendations.
Strong communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively with internal and external stakeholders.
Ability to work independently, manage priorities, and thrive in a remote, fast-paced environment.
Nice to have: exposure to SEO, landing page optimization, and multi-channel attribution models.


Benefits


Fully remote work setup with flexible working arrangements (part-time schedule).
Work-from-home support including company-provided equipment and connectivity assistance.
Exposure to international clients and global marketing environments.
Flexible work-life balance with a structured part-time schedule.
Opportunities for ongoing learning, professional development, and skill growth.
Collaborative, people-first culture that values autonomy and accountability.
Employee engagement activities, both virtual and in-person events.
Opportunity to directly impact campaign performance and measurable business growth.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

 Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

description:
About the job

Analista de Reingeniería de Procesos (Atizapán de Zaragoza, Estado de México) 







En BBVA llevamos más de 80 años trabajando para nuestros clientes. Nuestro propósito es poner al alcance de todos las oportunidades de esta nueva era. Este propósito refleja nuestro papel facilitador para ofrecer a nuestros clientes las mejores soluciones bancarias, ayudarles a tomar las mejores decisiones financieras e impactar positivamente en su vida




.

La Dirección General de Engineering es pieza fundamental dentro de la organización del Grupo BBVA México. Participamos como un solo equipo con las demás direcciones generales, aportando valor en la transformación de nuestro banco y de nuestro grupo, nuestra principal responsabilidad reside en desarrollar y operar soluciones tecnológicas y procesos seguros para mejorar la experiencia de nuestros clientes, ofrecer mejores productos y servicios y garantizarla continuidad operativa de nuestro banco.

Uno de los puestos clave para el alcance de nuestras metas es el de IT Project & Process Innovator. Te contamos más sobre las actividades de esta vacan

te:

● Diagramar los procesos internos y analizar los procesos actuales para identificar cue

llosde botella y eliminar redundancias.

ión.

● Automatizar e implementar controles digitales para disminuir los errores manuales y agilizar los flujos de trabajo.

tos.

● Liderar el seguimiento de hitos, gestionar riesgos y asegurar el cumplimiento de los entregables finales.

● Monitorear indicadores de rendimiento diario mediante KPIs.

● Alinear esfuerzos y gestionar equipos multidisciplinarios de diferentes áreas hacia un mismo entregable.

● Implementar procesos en las herramientas oficiales del banco.

● Garantizar los estándares de calidad definidos en los resultados de los proy

ectos.

● Establecer controles, trazabilidad y alertamientos oportunos ante los riesgos detectados.




Si te interesa formar parte de nuestro equipo, esta oportunidad es para ti.La vacante tiene un esquema laboral híbrido, con asistencia presencial los días martes y miércoles, con sede en CPD Lago Esmeralda, Atizapán de Zaragoza, Estado de México.

Competencias

● Mantener una comunicación asertiva y una eficaz capacidad de negociación.● Aplicar un pensamiento analítico y sistémico para desglosar problemas complejos.

● Trabajar en equipo y colaborar activamente por

description:
About the job

About Signify

Through bold discovery and cutting-edge innovation, we lead an industry that is vital for the future of our planet: lighting. Through our leadership in connected lighting and the Internet of Things, we're breaking new ground in data analytics, AI, and smart solutions for homes, offices, cities, and beyond.

At Signify, you can shape tomorrow by building on our incredible 125+ year legacy while working toward even bolder sustainability goals. Our culture of continuous learning, creativity, and commitment to diversity and inclusion empowers you to grow your skills and career.

Join us, and together, we’ll transform our industry, making a lasting difference for brighter lives and a better world.

More About The Role

We’re looking for an Intern to join our Consumer Channel team in Mexico.

Working for Signify means being creative and adaptive. Our culture of continuous learning and commitment to diversity and inclusion creates an environment that allows you to build your skills and career. Together, we’re transforming our industry.

As the world leader in lighting, we’re constantly ahead of the curve. Through our leadership in connected lighting and the Internet of Things, we’re breaking new grounds in data analytics, AI, and smart homes, offices, cities and more!

Signify is proud to be a leader in climate action, acting in line with the latest climate science. We are committed to reaching net zero greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions by 2040.

As an Intern / Apprentice you’ll be responsible for supporting Sales team and SOM role to improve customer service level in all the region (MEX & NOLA).

We’re on the lookout for forward-thinking innovators with a passion for sustainability. If you match this description, get in touch!

What You’ll Do


Sales Dashboard construction
Inventory level tracking (on hand / in transit) – Signify and Customer analysis
Slowmovers Action Tracker development
Sell In / Sell Out Consolidation ( 12 – 24 months)
Provisions report


Your Qualifications


Industrial Engineering or Business student (graduating after June 2027)
Intermediate English
Excel proficiency
Proactive


Our candidate profile is just a guide. We know that many talented people only apply for jobs when there is an exact match. If you don't fit all the criteria but believe this role is for you, we would very much like to hear from you.

We’re offering multiple opportunities for career development, like mentoring, coaching and stretch assignments, with strong international exposure for dynamic profiles. Learning is fundamental to our culture. Through continuous learning, we are transforming the industry. You have the chance to learn every day, acquire new skills and perspectives through customized online programs, and on-the-job experiences.

See #SignifyLife through the eyes of our employees!

Everything we’ll do for you

You can grow a lasting career here. We’ll encourage you, support you, and challenge you. We’ll help you learn and progress in a way that’s right for you, with coaching and mentoring along the way. We’ll listen to you too, because we see and value every one of our 27,000+ people. We believe that a diverse and inclusive workplace fosters creativity, innovation, and a full spectrum of bright ideas. With a global workforce present in 70+ countries, we are dedicated to creating an inclusive environment where every voice is heard and valued, helping us all achieve more together.

Come join us, and together we can light up the future.
